column2: 你辦不到，門鎖著，而你沒有鑰匙。

column1: You don't have time for that!
column2: 你沒有時間做這件事！

column1: You cautiously tap at the door, but are greeted only with vicious barking from the other side.
column2: 你小心翼翼地敲了敲門，回應你的只有門後傳來一陣兇猛的狗吠聲。

column1: You cautiously tap at the door. "JUST A MINUTE! I'M COMIN'!" a loud woman's voice yells. With a jerk, the door opens to reveal...oh no!...the ogress!!
column2: 你小心翼翼地敲了敲門。「等一下！我來了！」一個粗嗓門的女人喊道。門猛然一開，出現在你眼前的……糟了！……竟是女食人魔！！

column1: The door is already unlocked.
column2: 這扇門本來就沒上鎖。

column1: That's vandalism!
column2: 那是破壞公物的行為！

column1: You could never do that.
column2: 你絕不可能做那種事。

column1: The windows do not open.
column2: 這扇窗戶打不開。

column1: Poor Rosella! It looks like you'll join the deer in the stew pot tonight!
column2: 可憐的羅塞拉！看來你今晚要跟那隻鹿一起下鍋燉了！

column1: Oh, no! You're caught! The terrible ogre grabs you by the braids and drags you off to an untimely end. Dinner will definitely be on YOU tonight!
column2: 糟了！你被抓到了！那可怕的食人魔一把揪住你的辮子，把你拖向悲慘的結局。今晚的晚餐肯定就是你了！

column1: This is a creepy place! You have found a grove of very odd, (and scary-looking), trees. A thick forest surrounds the grove.
column2: 這是個令人毛骨悚然的地方！你發現一片古怪（而且看起來很恐怖）的樹林，四周被濃密的森林包圍。

column1: You see the back of a large, thatched-roof house.
column2: 你看到一間大茅草屋的後方。

column1: The door is at the front of the house.
column2: 大門在房子的正面。

column1: You look through the window, but can make out no details.
column2: 你透過窗戶往裡看，但看不清楚細節。

column1: Wooden you know it. You've been caught!
column2: 真沒想到，你被抓住了！

column1: You've really gotten yourself out on a limb this time, Rosella!
column2: 羅塞拉，這次你可真是深陷絕境了！

column1: Talk about being torn limb from limb!
column2: 真是把你大卸八塊了！

column1: Time to "leaf" this mortal world!
column2: 是時候「葉」開人世了！

column1: You've been a real sap this time, Rosella!
column2: 羅塞拉，這次你真是傻到家了！

column1: It seems you'll discover your roots, Rosella!
column2: 看來羅塞拉這次要「落地生根」了！

column1: You try to peer into the dark cave, but can make out no details.
column2: 你試著窺探漆黑的洞穴，但看不清楚裡面的細節。

column1: A cave, bearing a slight resemblance to a skull, juts out from the craggy mountainside. Huge rocks block your exit to the north, and you're completely surrounded by those grabby trees! Nice situation, huh?
column2: 一個略像骷髏頭的洞穴，從嶙峋的山壁凸出。巨石擋住了你北邊的去路，而你又被那些會抓人的樹團團圍住！這下處境還真不錯，是吧？

column1: The rocky skull cave juts out from the base of the steep mountainside. Smoke rises lazily from a large crack in the cave's (forehead?).
column2: 這座岩石骷髏洞從陡峭山壁的底部凸出，一縷輕煙緩緩地從洞穴（額頭？）的裂縫中升起。

column1: Even grass doesn't grow here.
column2: 這裡連草都長不出來。

column1: There are not many bushes or shrubs here.
column2: 這裡的灌木叢不多。

column1: Not many plants grow here; only those awful trees!
column2: 這裡長不出什麼植物，只有那些可怕的樹！

column1: There are no flowers here.
column2: 這裡沒有花。

column1: Those are vicious-looking trees, aren't they?
column2: 那些樹看起來很兇惡，對吧？

column1: There are large rocks all around you.
column2: 你的四周都是大岩石。

column1: In spite of these dreary surroundings, the sky is quite nice and blue.
column2: 儘管周圍景象陰森，天空倒是相當晴朗湛藍。

column1: The rocks are too large to climb.
column2: 這些岩石太大了，爬不上去。

column1: Large boulders block your way to the north.
column2: 巨大的岩石擋住了你北邊的去路。

column1: You see nothing interesting on the ground.
column2: 地上沒什麼有趣的東西。

column1: You've GOT to be KIDDING!
column2: 你一定是在開玩笑吧！

column1: That would be impossible.
column2: 那是不可能的。

column1: You couldn't get the fisherman.
column2: 你抓不到那個漁夫。

column1: What fisherman?
column2: 什麼漁夫？

column1: It's his, Rosella!
column2: 羅塞拉，那是他的東西！

column1: You're not a thief, Rosella!
column2: 羅塞拉，你不是小偷！

column1: You think, for a moment, that it might help, but then change your mind.
column2: 你想了一下，覺得這樣或許有幫助，但隨即打消了念頭。

column1: Who?
column2: 誰？

column1: The fisherman ignores you.
column2: 那個漁夫沒理你。

column1: How can you do that, Rosella?
column2: 羅塞拉，你怎麼能做那種事？

column1: A poor fisherman's shanty adorns this part of the coastline. A pier stretches, from the house, out into the ocean to the west. You see a pretty meadowland off to the east.
column2: 一間簡陋的漁夫小屋座落在這段海岸邊。一座碼頭從屋子向西延伸入海，東邊則能看到一片美麗的草地。

column1: An old, rusted anchor leans against the wall of the house.
column2: 一個生鏽的舊船錨靠在屋牆邊。

column1: The fisherman's shack looks badly in need of repair, as the sun, wind, and salt spray have taken their toll. From the house, an old pier leads out into the ocean.
column2: 這間漁夫小屋看起來急需整修，長年受風吹日曬和海水侵蝕已顯得破舊不堪。屋子旁有座老舊的碼頭通向大海。

column1: There is only ocean under the old pier.
column2: 老舊碼頭下方只有一片海水。

column1: This is a plain wooden door.
column2: 這是一扇樸素的木門。

column1: The old, worn pier juts out into the ocean from the weather-beaten house.
column2: 這座老舊磨損的碼頭，從飽經風霜的屋子向外延伸入海。

column1: You see a grizzled old fisherman on the pier. Apparently, he isn't having any luck today.
column2: 你看到碼頭上有個滿頭白髮的老漁夫，看樣子他今天運氣不太好。

column1: A pretty meadow leads eastward from here.
column2: 從這裡往東是一片美麗的草地。

column1: It's too heavy for you to move.
column2: 這東西太重了，你搬不動。

column1: The door is already closed.
column2: 這扇門已經關上了。

column1: You say a few words to the old fisherman. However, he must have other things on his mind as he seems to ignore you.
column2: 你對老漁夫說了幾句話，但他似乎心不在焉，沒理會你。

column1: You knock loudly on the shanty door. A woman's voice answers, "Jest come on in!"
column2: 你大聲敲了敲小屋的門，一個女人的聲音回應：「就直接進來吧！」

column1: You knock loudly on the shanty door. From inside, a man's voice calls out, "D'ya know what TIME it is?! GO AWAY!!"
column2: 你大聲敲了敲小屋的門，屋裡傳來一個男人的喊聲：「你知道現在幾點嗎？！滾開！！」

column1: Try waiting until it closes.
column2: 試著等到它關上再說。

column1: You can't see the door from here.
column2: 從這裡看不到那扇門。

column1: You see the back of an old shack.
column2: 你看到一間老舊小屋的後方。

column1: A beautiful green meadow, dotted with wildflowers, serves as the fisherman's spacious back yard. The flowers' sweet scent fills the air.
column2: 一片點綴著野花的美麗綠地，是漁夫家寬敞的後院，空氣中瀰漫著花朵的甜香。

column1: It's too difficult to climb while wearing that dress.
column2: 穿著那件洋裝，實在很難爬上去。

column1: It's too far in the distance to see it clearly.
column2: 距離太遠了，看不清楚。

column1: A large rock dominates the clearing.
column2: 一塊大岩石矗立在這片空地上。

column1: There are flowers here and there.
column2: 這裡到處都有花。

column1: You have entered a shady wooded area, with birds calling from the many trees. You notice a pool in the distance to the north.
column2: 你進入了一片陰涼的林地，許多樹上傳來鳥鳴聲。你注意到北方遠處有一座水池。

column1: A thick forest of pine trees surrounds you. You see a house in the distance to the north.
column2: 一片濃密的松林將你團團圍住，北方遠處可以看到一間房子。

column1: This place gives you the willies! The trees around here are very strange; almost human-like. A dense forest surrounds this odd grove of trees.
column2: 這地方讓你渾身發毛！周圍的樹木非常詭異，幾乎像人一樣。濃密的森林包圍著這片奇怪的樹林。

column1: You have come upon a bizarre group of scary, human-like trees. A thick forest of pine trees surrounds this strange group. Steep mountains block your way to the east.
column2: 你來到一群詭異又嚇人的樹前，它們幾乎像人一樣。濃密的松林包圍著這群怪樹，陡峭的山脈擋住了你東邊的去路。

column1: A green meadowland, dotted with clumps of wildflowers, leads eastward.
column2: 一片點綴著叢叢野花的綠地向東延伸。

column1: You see someone sitting on the end of the pier.
column2: 你看到有人坐在碼頭的盡頭。

column1: You see a shabby little house, with a pier, in the distance.
column2: 你在遠處看到一間破舊的小屋，還有一座碼頭。

column1: You can't make out who it is.
column2: 你看不清楚那是誰。

column1: You don't see anyone at the moment.
column2: 你目前沒看到任何人。

column1: A sparkling, white-sand beach edges a vast blue ocean. Turning to the east, you see a lush flowery meadow.
column2: 一片閃耀的白沙海灘緊鄰著蔚藍遼闊的大海。轉頭望向東邊，你看到一片繁花盛開的草地。

column1: The scent of wildflowers fills the air in the lovely green meadow.
column2: 在這片美麗的綠地上，空氣中瀰漫著野花的香氣。

column1: Yes, there's a tree stump in the middle of the meadow.
column2: 沒錯，草地中央有一個樹墩。

column1: This prince may be handsome, but he's also obnoxiously conceited.
column2: 這位王子雖然英俊，卻也自負得令人討厭。

column1: The prince does not answer you.
column2: 這位王子沒有回答你。

column1: You couldn't get the prince.
column2: 你抓不到這位王子。

column1: He's too conceited. You wouldn't want to.
column2: 他太自負了，你才不想那麼做。

column1: You can't see a prince here.
column2: 你在這裡看不到王子。

column1: You can't see any fish here.
column2: 你在這裡看不到任何魚。

column1: The large frog stares back at you with big bulging eyes.
column2: 那隻大青蛙瞪著一雙鼓凸的大眼睛回望著你。

column1: The water is too murky to see anything.
column2: 水太混濁了，什麼也看不見。

column1: The frog appears to be retrieving your gold ball.
column2: 那隻青蛙似乎正在幫你撿回金球。

column1: The frog sits calmly in the palm of your hand. Its big eyes stare into yours. A little gold crown rests upon its head.
column2: 青蛙靜靜地坐在你的手掌心，一雙大眼睛凝視著你，頭上還戴著一頂小小的金冠。

column1: This is a very pretty little pond. Floating upon it are many beautiful water lilies. %s
column2: 這是一座非常漂亮的小池塘，水面上漂浮著許多美麗的睡蓮。%s

column1: The frog is sitting with the gold ball at the east edge of the pond.
column2: 那隻青蛙正帶著金球坐在池塘東邊。

column1: Your golden ball is lying by the east edge of the pond.
column2: 你的金球就躺在池塘東邊。

column1: The frog is sitting on the ground near the edge of the pond.
column2: 那隻青蛙坐在池塘邊的地上。

column1: There is nothing of interest on the ground.
column2: 地上沒有什麼特別的東西。

column1: Many water lilies float upon this little pond. %s
column2: 這座小池塘上漂浮著許多睡蓮。%s

column1: It's a nice little gold crown.
column2: 這是一頂漂亮的小金冠。

column1: You see flowers here and there.
column2: 你看到四處點綴著花朵。

column1: Numerous water lilies float serenely upon the lovely little pond nestled in these woods. %s
column2: 這座座落在林中的美麗小池塘上，靜靜漂浮著許多睡蓮。%s

column1: It would waste your precious time to stop and pick flowers.
column2: 停下來摘花只會浪費你寶貴的時間。

column1: The water is too shallow.
column2: 水太淺了。

column1: Well, it's not wonderful, but it satisfies your thirst.
column2: 嗯，味道談不上美妙，但總算解了渴。

column1: You can't see the frog.
column2: 你看不到那隻青蛙。

column1: You're too far away from it.
column2: 你離它太遠了。

column1: Not while you're in the pond.
column2: 你人還在池塘裡，不能這麼做。

column1: You are too close to the water.
column2: 你離水太近了。

column1: Squinching up your nose in disgust, you catch the large frog and hold it in your hand. It stares at you with its big bulgy eyes, and wobbles its throat.
column2: 你皺著鼻子一臉嫌惡，抓起那隻大青蛙握在手中。牠瞪著一雙鼓凸的大眼睛看你，喉嚨還一鼓一鼓地抖動著。

column1: You're not near enough.
column2: 你離得不夠近。

column1: You have no need for a lily pad.
column2: 你用不著荷葉。

column1: Ribbit! Ribbit!
Well, what else did you expect from a frog?
column2: 呃，你還能對一隻青蛙有什麼期待？

column1: You "accidentally" drop the golden ball into the pond. From atop a lily pad, a large green frog leaps into the water, and disappears from view. A moment later, the frog emerges with...YES!...your lost ball in its mouth! It seems as if the frog is kindly returning your gold ball to you.
column2: 你「不小心」把金球掉進了池塘裡。一隻大青蛙從荷葉上一躍而下，跳進水中，消失了蹤影。片刻之後，青蛙浮出水面，嘴裡叼著……沒錯！……正是你遺失的金球！看來這隻青蛙是好心把金球還給你。

column1: Try another spot.
column2: 換個地方試試。

column1: That's not necessary any more.
column2: 現在已經不需要這麼做了。

column1: Not now.
column2: 現在還不行。

column1: You look at the frog's green lips. Mmmmm. Good. You feel silly doing this, as you slowly put your mouth against the frog's.
column2: 你看著青蛙那綠色的嘴唇。嗯……好吧。你覺得這麼做有點蠢，但還是慢慢把嘴湊了上去，貼上青蛙的嘴。

column1: First, you need to get the frog.
column2: 你得先抓到那隻青蛙才行。

column1: How can you do that?
column2: 你要怎麼做到？

column1: He is not interested.
column2: 他沒有興趣。

column1: There's no one here to give anything to.
column2: 這裡沒有人可以給東西。

column1: Ok.
column2: 好的。

column1: Oops, you scared the frog away!
column2: 糟糕，你把青蛙嚇跑了！

column1: Suddenly, the little green frog changes into a handsome prince!
column2: 突然間，那隻綠色的小青蛙變成了一位英俊的王子！

column1: Who are you? the prince demands. "I thought you were supposed to be a princess?! Why, you're nothing more than a PEASANT girl!" You blanch a bit at that remark, but say nothing.
column2: 「妳是誰？」王子質問道。「我還以為妳應該是位公主？！哼，妳根本只是個鄉下丫頭！」聽到這話，你臉色微微一沉，但什麼也沒說。

column1: Well, ta, ta, he says. "I'm off. Here, you may keep THIS!" To your surprise, he tosses the little golden crown to you as he takes his leave. Good riddance, you think.
column2: 「好啦，拜拜囉，」他說。「我要走了。喏，這個給妳留著！」讓你意外的是，他臨走前把那頂小金冠丟給了你。走了正好，你心想。

column1: You bend down and drink a bit of the pond water. Well, it's not wonderful, but it satisfies your thirst.
column2: 你彎下腰喝了點池塘裡的水。嗯，味道談不上美妙，但總算解了渴。

column1: Each tombstone has an epitaph written on it.
column2: 每塊墓碑上都刻著一段墓誌銘。

column1: 1643  Here lies Newberry Will, His life was finished 'cause he took ill, But none'll miss him, he should've been wiser, 'Tis his own fault, for bein' such a miser.
column2: 1643  這裡長眠著紐伯里·威爾，他因染病而英年早逝，沒有人會想念他，他本該更明智些，這全怪他自己，誰讓他如此吝嗇。

column1: Here lies the body of Mary Ann Lowder, She burst while drinking a seidlitz powder, Called from this world to her heavenly rest, She should have waited till it effervesced.
column2: 這裡長眠著瑪麗·安·勞德的遺體，她在喝發泡藥粉時脹裂身亡，就這樣被召喚離世、安息天堂，她其實該等藥粉完全化開再喝才對。

column1: To the ever living memory of Hiram Bennet, baby son of Edward and Sarah Bennet, who by a sudden-surprize fell asleep, the 11th day of November, 1553 Aged 6 months.
column2: 謹以此紀念永遠活在人們心中的海勒姆·班奈特，愛德華與莎拉·班奈特的幼子，於一五五三年十一月十一日猝然長眠，享年六個月。

column1: Six feet beneath This funeral wreath Is laid upon the shelf One Jerry Jones, Who dealt in bones, And now he's bones himself.
column2: 六呎深的地底下，這花圈之下安放著傑瑞·瓊斯，他生前以販骨為業，如今自己也成了一堆白骨。

column1: Lord Coningsby 1559 to 1626  Sleepeth here in peace: an honorable man, a valiant soldier. He served his country well.
column2: 科寧斯比爵士 一五五九年至一六二六年  安息於此：一位正直的人，一位英勇的戰士，他忠心為國效力。

column1: I plant these shrubs upon your grave, dear wife, That something on this spot may boast of life. Shrubs must whither and all earth must rot; Shrubs may revive: but you, thank heaven, will not.
column2: 親愛的妻子，我在妳的墳前種下這些灌木，好讓這方寸之地也能展現一絲生機。灌木終將枯萎，塵土終將腐朽；灌木尚可重生，但妳，謝天謝地，卻不會。

column1: Beneath this stone, a lump of clay, Lies Uncle Peter Dan'els, Who, early in the month of May, Took off his winter flannels.
column2: 這塊石頭之下，一抔黃土，長眠著彼得·丹尼爾斯叔叔，五月初的某一天，他脫下了冬天的絨衣。

column1: Dr. I Letsome  When people's ill they come to I, I physics, bleeds, and sweats 'em; Sometimes they live, Sometimes they die; What's that to I? I Letsome.
column2: 萊特森醫生 人們生病了就來找我，我開藥、放血、發汗醫治他們；有時他們活了下來，有時他們死了；那與我何干？我是萊特森。

column1: Here I lie with my three daughters, Who died drinking Chelt'nam waters. If we had stuck to Epsom Salt, We should not sleep in this cold vault.
column2: 我與我的三個女兒長眠於此，她們死於飲用切爾滕納姆礦泉水。若我們當初堅持喝瀉鹽水，就不必如今長眠在這冰冷的墓穴中。

column1: Strong and athletic was my frame; Far from my native home I came, And manly fought with Simon Byrne; Alas! but liv'd not to return.  Reader, take warning by my fate, Lest you should rue your case too late; If you have ever fought before, Determine now to fight no more.
column2: 我曾體格強健、身手矯捷；我遠離故鄉來到此地，堂堂正正與賽門·拜恩一戰；唉！卻未能活著歸去。讀者啊，引以為戒吧，免得你日後追悔莫及；若你曾經與人爭鬥，從今起就此罷手吧。

column1: Thorp's Corpse.
column2: 索普的屍骸。

column1: There is nothing of importance in the old tree.
column2: 這棵老樹裡沒有什麼重要的東西。

column1: One of the graves is marked by a cross.
column2: 其中一座墳墓上立著一個十字架。

column1: An old, rotting tree adds appropriate charm to the decrepit cemetery.
column2: 一棵腐朽的老樹，為這座破敗的墓園增添了幾分應景的陰森氣息。

column1: The hole is empty.
column2: 這個洞是空的。

column1: This run-down, gloomy cemetery gives you the creeps! Many of the old tombstones have fallen over, and the entire place is overgrown with grass and weeds.
column2: 這座破敗陰森的墓園讓你毛骨悚然！許多古老的墓碑已經傾倒，整個地方都長滿了雜草。

column1: Try somewhere else. The ground is too hard here.
column2: 換個地方試試，這裡的地面太硬了。

column1: You don't know what to dig for...yet.
column2: 你還不知道自己要挖什麼……至少現在還不知道。

column1: You have nothing to dig with.
column2: 你沒有可以用來挖掘的工具。

column1: The terrifying zombie senses your obsidian scarab. It immediately retreats and goes back to whence it came.
column2: 那隻可怕的殭屍察覺到你身上的黑曜石聖甲蟲，立刻退了回去，回到牠原本的地方。

column1: To wander forever is now your fate.
column2: 從此永遠遊蕩，就是你的命運。

column1: Oh, Oh! You've broken your shovel!
column2: 噢，糟了！你的鏟子壞掉了！

column1: You dig a small hole at the grave of the old miser. Aha! You have found a bag of gold coins!
column2: 你在那個老吝嗇鬼的墳前挖了個小洞。啊哈！你找到了一袋金幣！

column1: You carefully dig a small hole at the poor baby's grave. What is this?! You have uncovered a silver baby rattle!
column2: 你小心翼翼地在那可憐嬰兒的墳前挖了個小洞。這是什麼？！你挖出了一個銀製的嬰兒搖鈴！

column1: You dig a small hole at the grave of the Lord of the Manor. Gotcha! You have found an impressive Medal of Honor!
column2: 你在莊園領主的墳前挖了個小洞。找到了！你發現了一枚氣派的榮譽勳章！

column1: You dig a small hole...but find nothing of interest.
column2: 你挖了個小洞……但什麼有趣的東西也沒找到。

column1: You bend down, pick it up, and carry it with you.
column2: 你彎下腰把它撿起來，隨身帶著。

column1: Indeed, this is a spooky old house! It looks as if no one has lived here for many years. Flanking the old house on both sides is a run-down cemetery. A thick forest looms all around.
column2: 這確實是一棟陰森的老房子！看起來已經多年無人居住。老房子兩側都是荒廢的墓園，四周被茂密的森林所籠罩。

column1: The graves are in the cemetery.
column2: 那些墳墓就在墓園裡。

column1: An old, crumbling stone wall surrounds the neglected cemetery.
column2: 一道古老崩塌的石牆，環繞著這座荒廢的墓園。

column1: This is not a pleasant house; in fact, it's downright scary! It looks old and abandoned, and badly in need of tender-loving-care. To make matters worse, it sits right in the middle of an old cemetery!
column2: 這絕不是一棟討人喜歡的房子；事實上，簡直可怕極了！它看起來又老又荒廢，急需好好整修。更糟的是，它就座落在一座古老墓園的正中央！

column1: A decrepit and over-grown cemetery surrounds the old house.
column2: 一座破敗荒蕪、雜草叢生的墓園，環繞著這棟老房子。

column1: This is a finely-carved, wooden door.
column2: 這是一扇雕工精美的木門。

column1: The bushes are too tall! Go up by the door.
column2: 灌木叢太高了！從門邊過去吧。

column1: Wide stone steps lead up to the front door of the spooky old house.
column2: 寬闊的石階通往這棟陰森老房子的正門。

column1: Overgrown shrubs front the old house.
column2: 老房子前長滿了雜亂的灌木叢。

column1: It looks like many of the windows are already broken!
column2: 看起來有許多窗戶早就破掉了！

column1: The door is closed.
column2: 門是關著的。

column1: With trepidation, you rap on the door. Not surprisingly, nobody answers.
column2: 你戰戰兢兢地敲了敲門。不出所料，沒有人應門。

column1: You're not interested in climbing rocks.
column2: 你對爬石頭沒興趣。

column1: The door is open; just enter it.
column2: 門開著，直接進去就行了。

column1: Try opening the crypt door.
column2: 試著打開墓室的門吧。

column1: There are rocks here and there.
column2: 這裡到處都是石頭。

column1: Tufts of grass and weeds sprout around the old tombstones.
column2: 一叢叢雜草從古老的墓碑周圍冒了出來。

column1: The stone crypt is built into the mountainside!
column2: 這座石造墓室是嵌建在山壁裡的！

column1: A stone crypt is built into the mountainside.
column2: 一座石造墓室嵌建在山壁裡。

column1: An interesting statue of a lion is mounted above the crypt door.
column2: 墓室門上方立著一座造型有趣的獅子雕像。

column1: The crypt door is closed.
column2: 墓室的門關著。

column1: The crypt door is open.
column2: 墓室的門開著。

column1: You don't see it here.
column2: 你在這裡沒看到它。

column1: This is a scary old cemetery! Many of the tombstones are crumbling or falling over, and the entire place is overgrown with weeds. A mountain range looms to the east, and all around a forest encroaches.
column2: 這是一座陰森的老墓園！許多墓碑已經崩裂或傾倒，整個地方都長滿了雜草。東邊聳立著一片山脈，四周則被森林步步進逼。

column1: How odd! You see a crypt door built right into the mountainside!
column2: 真奇怪！你看到一扇嵌建在山壁裡的墓室門！

column1: You pound on the door and hear your knocks echo from within. There is no answer.
column2: 你用力敲門，聽見敲擊聲在裡頭迴盪。但沒有人回應。

column1: The door is locked.
column2: 門是鎖著的。

column1: The crypt door is already open.
column2: 墓室的門已經開著了。

column1: You place the skeleton key in the lock of the crypt door. Click. It locked! You kick the key through a crack under the door. Now no one will ever accidentally release the evils of Pandora's box into the world!
column2: 你把萬能鑰匙插進墓室門的鎖孔裡。喀嚓一聲，鎖上了！你把鑰匙從門縫下踢了進去。這樣一來，再也不會有人不小心把潘朵拉的盒子的邪惡釋放到世上了！

column1: Ok. You lock the door.
column2: 好的。你把門鎖上了。

column1: First you need to close the door.
column2: 你得先把門關上才行。

column1: The door is already locked.
column2: 門已經鎖上了。

column1: You don't have the right key to lock this door.
column2: 你沒有能鎖上這扇門的鑰匙。

column1: You place the skeleton key in the lock of the crypt door. Slowly, you turn it. Click. It unlocked!
column2: 你把萬能鑰匙插進墓室門的鎖孔裡，慢慢轉動。喀嚓一聲，解鎖了！

column1: You don't have the key to unlock this door.
column2: 你沒有能打開這扇門的鑰匙。

column1: The door is already open.
column2: 門已經開了。

column1: Betty Cowden 1650 to 1669  Here lieth the body of Betty Cowden Who would live longer but she couden; Sorrow and grief made her decay When she lost her lover at sea one day.
column2: 貝蒂·考登 一六五零年至一六六九年  這裡安息著貝蒂·考登的遺體，她本該活得更久，卻未能如願；當她某日在海上失去了愛人，悲傷與哀慟使她日漸憔悴。

column1: Whoe'er you are, tread softly, I entreat you, For if he chance to wake, be sure he'll eat you.
column2: 無論你是誰，我懇求你輕輕地走路，因為萬一他醒了過來，他一定會把你吃掉。

column1: At length, my friends, the feast of life is o'er, I've eat sufficient, and I'll drink no more; My night is come, I've spent a jovial day, 'Tis time to part, but oh!--what is to pay?
column2: 終於，朋友們啊，人生的盛宴已經結束，我吃得夠多了，也不會再喝了；我的長夜已經降臨，這一天過得歡快，是時候告別了，但唉！——代價又是什麼呢？

column1: 1546  Reader, here lies--but forbear To read more without a tear, One--I cannot speak the rest, You may weep. I'll smite my breast, Grief preventing, and this stone, Too small to be written on. Only this--a little boy, Willy--in Abram's bosom's laid.
column2: 一五四六年  讀者啊，這裡長眠著——但請忍住，別不掉淚地讀下去，有一位——我說不下去了，你儘管流淚吧。我將捶胸頓足，止不住悲傷，而這塊墓碑，太小了，容不下更多的字。只寫著這些——一個小男孩，威利——安息在亞伯拉罕的懷抱中。

column1: Rebecca Freeland 1598  She drank good ale, good punch and wine And lived to the age of 99.
column2: 麗貝卡·弗里蘭 一五九八年  她喝上好的麥酒、賓治酒與葡萄酒，一直活到九十九歲。

column1: 1634 to 1672  She done her best.
column2: 一六三四年至一六七二年  她已經盡力了。

column1: Here lies a poor woman who was always tired, She lived in a house where help wasn't hired: Her last words on earth were: "Dear friends, I am going To where there's no cooking, or washing, or sewing, For everything there is exact to my wishes, For where they don't eat there's no washing of dishes, I'll be where loud anthems will always be ringing, But having no voice I'll be quit of the singing, Don't mourn for me now, don't mourn for me never, I am going to do nothing for ever and ever."
column2: 這裡長眠著一位總是疲憊不堪的可憐女人，她住的屋子裡從沒請過幫傭：她在世上的最後一句話是：「親愛的朋友們，我要去一個不用煮飯、不用洗衣、也不用縫紉的地方了，那裡一切都稱心如意，因為沒有人吃東西，也就不必洗碗盤，我將置身於響亮聖歌長鳴之處，但因為我沒有嗓子，倒也用不著跟著唱，別為我哀傷，現在不要，永遠也不要，我將永永遠遠什麼事也不做。」

column1: Dentist Brown  Stranger! Approach this spot with gravity! John Brown is filling his last cavity.
column2: 布朗牙醫 陌生人啊！請以莊重的心情靠近此地！約翰·布朗正在填補他人生最後一顆蛀牙。

column1: Oh, oh! You've broken your shovel!
column2: 噢，糟了！你的鏟子壞掉了！

column1: You dig a small hole at the little boy ghost's grave. Aha! You have uncovered a toy horse!
column2: 你在那個小男孩幽靈的墳前挖了個小洞。啊哈！你挖出了一匹玩具馬！

column1: You carefully dig a small hole at the sad lady ghost's grave. Yea! You have found a lovely locket!
column2: 你小心翼翼地在那位悲傷女幽靈的墳前挖了個小洞。太好了！你找到了一個可愛的墜飾盒！

column1: You dig a small hole...but don't find anything of interest.
column2: 你挖了個小洞……但什麼有趣的東西也沒找到。

column1: A pretty meadow leads eastward.
column2: 一片美麗的草原向東延伸。

column1: Better be careful around the cliffs!
column2: 在懸崖附近最好小心點！

column1: Wistfully, you stare out over the ocean, wishing you could go home and see your family again. Unfortunately, reality sets in, and you remember your difficult situation. Turning your head, you look eastward toward a meadowland covered with wildflowers, and heave a big sigh of sadness.
column2: 你若有所思地望著遠方的大海，多麼希望能回家再見到家人一面。可惜現實終究浮現，你想起了自己目前的困境。你轉過頭，望向東邊那片開滿野花的草原，重重地嘆了一口氣。

column1: You really should be more careful, Rosella!
column2: 妳真的該小心一點，羅塞拉！

column1: That last step was a doozy!
column2: 剛才那一步可真夠嗆的！

column1: Watch where you're going, Rosella!
column2: 看清楚妳要走的路，羅塞拉！

column1: That last step was a real doozy!
column2: 剛才那一步真是驚險萬分！

column1: Oops, you slipped!
column2: 哎呀，妳滑倒了！

column1: A green meadowland, filled with beautiful wildflowers, surrounds you.
column2: 一片綠色的草原環繞著你，開滿了美麗的野花。

column1: You peek under the bridge, but do not see anything of importance from where you're standing.
column2: 你朝橋下窺探，但從你站的地方看不出有什麼重要的東西。

column1: A rustic stone bridge adds a bit of charm to this part of the woods.
column2: 一座古樸的石橋，為這片林地增添了幾分風情。

column1: A gentle stream meanders its way through the trees. A rustic stone bridge crosses its path.
column2: 一條潺潺的小溪蜿蜒穿過林間，一座古樸的石橋橫跨其上。

column1: You kneel down and peer under the bridge. Aha! You have found a small golden ball! You pick it up and carry it with you.
column2: 你跪下身，往橋下窺探。啊哈！你找到了一顆小金球！你把它撿起來，隨身帶著。

column1: You see nothing of importance under the bridge.
column2: 橋下沒有什麼重要的東西。

column1: The roots of this great tree envelop a little, snug house. Smoke lazily curls from its chimney, and an old rustic water wheel rests beside the river.
column2: 這棵大樹的樹根環抱著一間溫馨的小屋。裊裊炊煙從煙囪緩緩升起，一座老舊的水車靜靜佇立在河邊。

column1: It's a small wooden door.
column2: 這是一扇小小的木門。

column1: That would not be a good idea right now.
column2: 現在這麼做恐怕不是個好主意。

column1: You can't. You don't have the key.
column2: 你辦不到，你沒有鑰匙。

column1: Go away!
column2: 「走開！」

column1: You knock loudly on the small door and hear a shuffling from inside. A sleepy voice calls out, "We're all asleep here! Come back tomorrow!"
column2: 你大力敲了敲小門，聽見屋裡傳來一陣窸窸窣窣的聲響。一個睡意濃厚的聲音喊道：「我們都睡了！明天再來吧！」

column1: You knock loudly on the small door. No answer.
column2: 你大力敲了敲小門，沒有人回應。

column1: The water wheel appears run-down, and is currently not in use.
column2: 這座水車看起來已經荒廢，目前並未使用。

column1: You have come upon a cute little house, built right into a huge tree! An old water wheel, attached to the house, goes unused beside the little river that flows gently before the house.
column2: 你來到一間可愛的小屋前，它就建在一棵大樹之中！屋旁一座老水車閒置不用，靜靜立在屋前緩緩流淌的小河邊。

column1: ...and STAY OUT!
column2: 「……別再回來了！」

column1: You are walking through a forest thick with pine trees. A nearby river rushes westward from the distant mountain range. In the distance, you see a large house.
column2: 你走在一片松樹茂密的森林裡。附近一條河流從遠方山脈奔騰而下，向西流去。遠處，你看見一棟大房子。

column1: A pretty robin pulls hungrily at a long earthworm.
column2: 一隻漂亮的知更鳥正貪婪地啄著一條長長的蚯蚓。

column1: You see a bird flying away.
column2: 你看見一隻鳥飛走了。

column1: Cheep, cheep!
Not a little robin!
column2: 別對這隻小知更鳥這麼做！

column1: The robin can't understand.
column2: 知更鳥聽不懂。

column1: There's no one here.
column2: 這裡沒有人。

column1: You see a fat worm wiggling on the ground.
column2: 你看見一條肥蚯蚓在地上蠕動。

column1: You can't see through the waterfall.
column2: 你看不穿瀑布。

column1: You can't make out many details from here.
column2: 從這裡看不清楚細節。

column1: You look under the water, but can see nothing.
column2: 你查看水底，但什麼也看不見。

column1: You look into the water, but see nothing of interest.
column2: 你望進水中，沒看見什麼特別的東西。

column1: You think you see something behind the waterfall, but you're not sure what it is.
column2: 你隱約覺得瀑布後面好像有什麼東西，但看不清楚是什麼。

column1: This is a magnificent waterfall! It tumbles into a deep pool, which then flows westward as a rushing river.
column2: 這是一座壯觀的瀑布！水流傾瀉而下，注入一座深潭，再化作奔騰的河水向西流去。

column1: You peer into the pool of water, but cannot see anything of importance.
column2: 你凝視潭水，但沒發現什麼重要的東西。

column1: You see a deep pool below the waterfall.
column2: 你看見瀑布下方有一座深潭。

column1: You see a beautiful waterfall cascading down the mountain into a deep blue pool. From the pool, a river courses westward. Around you, a dense forest closes in.
column2: 你看見一道美麗的瀑布從山上傾瀉而下，注入一座湛藍的深潭。潭水化作河流向西奔流，四周被茂密的森林環繞著。

column1: The force of the water is too strong for you to be able to do that.
column2: 水流太強勁了，你辦不到。

column1: You're not strong enough. The current forces you back!
column2: 你力氣不夠，水流把你逼退了回去！

column1: The water isn't deep enough here.
column2: 這裡的水不夠深。

column1: You'd have to be in the water to do that.
column2: 你得先下水才能這麼做。

column1: You are already swimming.
column2: 你已經在游泳了。

column1: You are already in the water.
column2: 你已經在水裡了。

column1: Not while you're IN the water.
column2: 你人在水裡時可不能這麼做。

column1: The force of the water pushes you back.
column2: 水流的力量把你推了回去。

column1: The cold water of the river contrasts sharply with the warmer ocean water as the two converge.
column2: 河水冰涼，與較溫暖的海水交會時形成強烈對比。

column1: Eastward, you see a pretty green meadow.
column2: 往東望去，你看見一片美麗的翠綠草原。

column1: A river, coming from the east, spills into the blue ocean before you. A lonely beach edges the ocean. From atop the bluff, a lovely meadow stretches eastward.
column2: 一條河流從東方而來，注入你眼前湛藍的海洋。一片孤寂的沙灘沿著海岸延伸，從懸崖上望去，一片美麗的草原向東延展開來。

column1: Ahhh! It tastes good!
column2: 啊∼味道真好！

column1: A cold river carves its way through this lush flowery meadow.
column2: 一條冰涼的河流蜿蜒穿過這片花草繁茂的草原。

column1: You are wandering through a thick wood. %s
column2: 你漫步在一片茂密的樹林裡。%s

column1: The door is wide open.
column2: 這扇門大大敞開著。

column1: You look inside, but can make out no details.
column2: 你往裡面看，但看不清楚任何細節。

column1: The crude mine is carved into a small hill. Its solid, wooden door is propped open.
column2: 這座簡陋的礦坑鑿在一座小山丘裡，堅固的木門敞開著。

column1: You see a crude mine shaft in the midst of this forest of pine trees.

column1: You couldn't get the fisherman's wife.
column2: 你沒辦法把漁夫的妻子帶走。

column1: You don't need it.
column2: 你用不著它。

column1: You can't.
column2: 你辦不到。

column1: It's firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 它被牢牢固定在牆上。

column1: You introduce yourself to the old fisherman's wife. With little patience, she responds, "What're you doin' here, young lady?! Can't you see I'm busy?! We have enough problems without worryin' 'bout you!"
column2: 你向老漁夫的妻子自我介紹。她不耐煩地回應：「妳來這裡做什麼，小姑娘？！沒看到我正忙著嗎？！我們的麻煩已經夠多了，不需要再為妳操心！」

column1: You try to talk pleasantly to the tired-looking woman, but your words fall on deaf ears. She is obviously annoyed by your presence.
column2: 你試著和這位疲憊的婦人好好說話，但她根本聽不進去，顯然對你的出現感到不耐。

column1: I said GIT, and I mean GIT!
column2: 「我說滾，我是說真的滾！」

column1: You say hello to the poor woman. With little patience, she responds, "What're you doin' in here, young lady?! Can't you see I'm busy?! We have enough problems without worryin' 'bout you!"
column2: 你向這位貧苦的婦人打招呼。她不耐煩地回應：「妳來這裡做什麼，小姑娘？！沒看到我正忙著嗎？！我們的麻煩已經夠多了，不需要再為妳操心！」

column1: You try to talk pleasantly to the tired-looking woman, but your words seem to fall on deaf ears. She is obviously annoyed by your presence.
column2: 你試著和這位疲憊的婦人好好說話，但她似乎根本聽不進去，顯然對你的出現感到不耐。

column1: You speak to the fisherman's wife. She smiles as she replies, "You're a very kind girl. You've helped us out a lot. Why, I might even be able ta buy some bran' new clothes!"
column2: 你和漁夫的妻子說話。她微笑著回答：「妳真是個善良的姑娘，妳幫了我們許多忙。哎呀，我說不定還能買件全新的衣裳呢！」

column1: You say a few words to the woman, but she seems not to hear you as she is humming softly to herself while %s.
column2: 你對這位婦人說了幾句話，但她似乎沒聽見，只是一邊 %s 一邊輕輕哼著歌。

column1: You speak to the old fisherman, and he replies, "You've really helped me and the missus, girlie! Those diamonds will come in awful handy. We can't thank ya enough!"
column2: 你和老漁夫說話，他回答：「小姑娘，妳真是幫了我和老婆一個大忙！那些鑽石可派上大用場了。我們真是感激不盡！」

column1: Again, you say a few words to the fisherman. "All right, already!" he exclaims. "I told ya that I was grateful! Don't you have somethin' else ta do?"
column2: 你又對漁夫說了幾句話。「好啦好啦！」他大聲說，「我不是說過我很感激了嗎！妳沒別的事好做嗎？」

column1: You talk to the grizzled fisherman as he sits at the table. Sighing, he tells you, "Them fish ain't been bitin' lately. If things don't git better soon, I don't know WHAT to do!"
column2: 你和坐在桌邊的老漁夫說話。他嘆了口氣，對你說：「這陣子魚都不上鉤。要是情況再不好轉，我真不知道該怎麼辦才好！」

column1: In reply, the old fisherman comments, "Me and the wife ain't gettin' any younger, and times is tough. If you've got any ideas, girlie, let me know."
column2: 老漁夫回答道：「我和老婆年紀都不小了，日子也不好過。小姑娘，妳要是有什麼點子，儘管告訴我。」

column1: The fisherman chooses to ignore your attempted conversation. Instead, he looks dismally into his coffee cup and sighs heavily.
column2: 漁夫選擇不理會你的搭話，只是黯然地盯著手中的咖啡杯，重重嘆了口氣。

column1: You have found yourself stranded upon a desert island. Also, it seems, have others before you. Now, what are you going to do, Rosella?!
column2: 你發現自己被困在一座荒島上，看來在你之前也有其他人淪落至此。羅塞拉，現在你打算怎麼做？！

column1: You're already swimming.
column2: 你已經在游泳了。

column1: Just go deeper.
column2: 再往深處游就是了。

column1: Just go and do it.
column2: 直接去做就是了。

column1: YUCK!! This water's too salty!
column2: 噁！這水也太鹹了！

column1: You can't see it from here.
column2: 你從這裡看不到它。

column1: A bottle-nose dolphin pokes its nose out of the water and chatters noisily.
column2: 一隻瓶鼻海豚把鼻子探出水面，吱吱喳喳地叫個不停。

column1: The small island is nothing more than a sand bar, decorated with two lonely palm trees...and various shipwrecks.
column2: 這座小島不過是一片沙洲，點綴著兩棵孤零零的棕櫚樹……還有幾艘破船殘骸。

column1: You're not in the water.
column2: 你不在水裡。

column1: You look under the water, and see nothing but...more water.
column2: 你往水底下看，除了水……什麼也沒有。

column1: The blue ocean surrounds this small island. You look in all directions, but can see no sign of land.
column2: 蔚藍的海洋環繞著這座小島。你四處張望，卻看不見任何陸地的跡象。

column1: In this case, the beach and the island are one and the same!
column2: 在這裡，沙灘和小島根本就是同一個地方！

column1: You see a shiny whistle lying on the ground.
column2: 你看見地上躺著一個閃亮的哨子。

column1: You look at the ground and see nothing but sand.
column2: 你看向地面，除了沙子什麼也沒有。

column1: Only one lonely palm grows here.
column2: 這裡只長著一棵孤零零的棕櫚樹。

column1: You don't see any coconuts.
column2: 你沒看見任何椰子。

column1: It looks as if many an unlucky sailor has been stranded here.
column2: 看來曾有不少倒楣的水手在此擱淺。

column1: A pelican visits you on this desert island.
column2: 一隻鵜鶘造訪了這座荒島上的你。

column1: It is difficult to climb trees while wearing that dress!
column2: 穿著那件洋裝很難爬樹！

column1: Try doing that somewhere else.
column2: 換個地方再試試吧。

column1: Not while the pelican is flying.
column2: 鵜鶘還在飛的時候不行。

column1: You've already done that.
column2: 你已經做過了。

column1: You don't have one.
column2: 你沒有這個東西。

column1: You already have it.
column2: 你已經擁有它了。

column1: Okay. TWEEEEEEEEEEEET!
column2: 好的。嗶——！

column1: You feel silly trying to talk to a pelican!
column2: 你試著跟鵜鶘說話，感覺自己傻透了！

column1: Feed it what?
column2: 要餵它什麼？

column1: You would have to catch the pelican first.
column2: 你得先抓住那隻鵜鶘才行。

column1: You can try.
column2: 你可以試試看。

column1: The pelican has no interest in it.
column2: 鵜鶘對它沒有興趣。

column1: The dolphin has no interest in it.
column2: 海豚對它沒有興趣。

column1: Please be more specific.
column2: 請說清楚一點。

column1: You can't "get" the dolphin. You could try riding it.
column2: 你沒辦法「拿走」海豚，不過你可以試著騎上牠。

column1: You are RIDING the dolphin!
column2: 你正騎在海豚背上！

column1: You look at the ground inside the wrecked boat. What is this?! Why, it's a golden bridle! You pick it up and carry it with you.
column2: 你看向破船內部的地面。這是什麼？！是一副金色的馬轡！你把它撿起來隨身帶著。

column1: You throw the stinky, dead fish to the pelican. Greedily, he catches it in his large bill. As he does so, you notice something shiny fall from his bill, onto the ground.
column2: 你把那條發臭的死魚扔給鵜鶘。牠貪婪地用大喙接住了魚。就在此時，你注意到有個閃亮的東西從牠嘴裡掉了出來，落到地上。

column1: You put the shiny, silver whistle to your lips and forcefully blow into it, producing a loud TWEEEEEEEEEEET! To your astonishment, a friendly dolphin appears just off-shore and jabbers excitedly at you.
column2: 你把那個閃亮的銀色哨子湊到嘴邊，用力吹了一聲，發出響亮的嗶——！讓你驚訝的是，一隻友善的海豚出現在不遠的岸邊，興奮地對你吱吱叫著。

column1: It looks like exposure and thirst have done you in, Rosella.
column2: 羅塞拉，看來曝曬和乾渴終究要了你的命。

column1: Horrors! You have been swallowed by a huge whale!
column2: 太可怕了！你被一頭巨鯨吞下了肚！

column1: PHEW!! This place stinks!
column2: 呼！這地方臭死了！

column1: There is nothing of importance with the skeleton.
column2: 這副骸骨身上沒有什麼重要的東西。

column1: NO!!
column2: 不行！！

column1: This old boat won't help you.
column2: 這艘舊船幫不了你。

column1: What bottle?
column2: 什麼瓶子？

column1: You couldn't start a fire in here as it's too wet.
column2: 這裡太潮濕了，你沒辦法生火。

column1: Bits and pieces of fish are everywhere.
column2: 到處都是魚的碎屑殘渣。

column1: There's nothing in the old boat. It didn't help "James" and it won't help you.
column2: 這艘舊船裡什麼都沒有。它幫不了「詹姆士」，也幫不了你。

column1: There is nothing of interest under the seawater.
column2: 海水底下沒有什麼值得注意的東西。

column1: This is a disgusting place to be! Seawater, dead fish, junk...you name it...float around in here. Hopefully, your fate will not be the same as poor "James" lying over there.
column2: 這裡實在噁心透了！海水、死魚、雜物……什麼都有……漂浮在四周。但願你的命運不會和躺在那邊的可憐「詹姆士」一樣。

column1: This is the biggest tongue you have ever seen! It's also extremely slippery, slimy, and bumpy. Ugh!
column2: 這是你見過最大的舌頭！又滑又黏還凹凸不平，噁！

column1: The whale's throat and mouth are enormous! Huge teeth, a great tongue, and yes...even a large uvula. What's that?, you say. Well, suffice it to say, it looks like a big punching bag and hangs down from the top of the throat. See, you learn something new every day!
column2: 這頭鯨魚的喉嚨和嘴巴大得驚人！有巨大的牙齒、碩大的舌頭，沒錯……甚至還有一顆碩大的懸雍垂。妳問那是什麼？這麼說吧，它看起來就像個大沙包，從喉嚨頂端垂掛下來。瞧，人每天都能學到新東西！

column1: Yes, that's a big uvula, all right!
column2: 沒錯，那的確是個碩大的懸雍垂！

column1: The skeleton of an unlucky man (let's call him James), rests atop the wreckage of a small boat.
column2: 一具倒楣男子的骸骨（就叫他詹姆士吧）躺在小船殘骸上。

column1: You notice a bottle floating in the seawater of the whale's stomach.
column2: 你注意到鯨魚胃裡的海水中漂著一個瓶子。

column1: Seawater floats in the whale's stomach.
column2: 鯨魚的胃裡積著海水。

column1: You strike the whale's teeth...but nothing happens.
column2: 你敲了敲鯨魚的牙齒……但什麼事也沒發生。

column1: You strike the whale's tongue...but nothing happens.
column2: 你敲了敲鯨魚的舌頭……但什麼事也沒發生。

column1: You strike the large uvula (punching bag-looking thing)...but nothing happens.
column2: 你敲了敲那顆碩大的懸雍垂（那個像沙包的東西）……但什麼事也沒發生。

column1: Go ahead. Try it.
column2: 來吧，試試看。

column1: You scream, holler, and shout for help until your throat is hoarse; but to no avail.
column2: 你聲嘶力竭地大喊、呼救，直到喉嚨都啞了，卻依然徒勞無功。

column1: How could you open the whale's mouth?
column2: 你要怎麼撬開鯨魚的嘴？

column1: You really want to swim here?!
column2: 你真的想在這裡游泳？！

column1: You're not strong enough to do that.
column2: 你的力氣不夠做這件事。

column1: Not here!
column2: 這裡不行！

column1: You frantically tickle the throat of the whale with the big peacock feather. You begin to feel a quiver under your feet, which quickly turns into a mighty tremble. Suddenly, you hear a thunderous AAAAACCCCHHHHOOOOOOOO!, and you are swept out of the whale's mouth, in a tremendous wave of ocean water!
column2: 你用那根大孔雀羽毛拚命搔弄鯨魚的喉嚨。你感覺到腳下開始震動，很快變成了猛烈的顫抖。突然間，你聽見一聲震天的哈啾——！接著就被一股巨大的海水浪潮沖出了鯨魚的嘴巴！

column1: It looks like the noxious fumes have finally gotten to you, Rosella.
column2: 羅塞拉，看來這些毒氣終究還是讓你倒下了。

column1: Genesta is very ill. She lies, unmoving, in her shell bed.
column2: 吉妮絲病得很重，一動也不動地躺在貝殼床上。

column1: Genesta's magic wand lies upon the bed.
column2: 吉妮絲的魔杖放在床上。

column1: You can't see out the window from here.
column2: 你從這裡看不到窗外。

column1: Gorgeous flowers surround Genesta as she lies, ill, on her bed.
column2: 吉妮絲病臥床上，四周環繞著美麗的花朵。

column1: Wide steps lead down from Genesta's bed chamber.
column2: 寬闊的階梯從吉妮絲的寢室通往樓下。

column1: No. It's private.
column2: 不行，這是私人物品。

column1: It must hold Genesta's clothes.
column2: 這裡面應該放著吉妮絲的衣物。

column1: There is nothing of importance on the walls.
column2: 牆上沒有什麼重要的東西。

column1: There is nothing interesting on the floor.
column2: 地板上沒有什麼有趣的東西。

column1: The lovely fairy is visibly fading...fast. Within hours, if she doesn't get her magic talisman back, she'll be dead. %s
column2: 美麗的仙女正明顯地虛弱下去……而且很快。再過幾個小時，如果她拿不回魔法護身符，就會死去。%s

column1: A white, spotted snow leopard lies on the floor of Genesta's bed chamber. It lies quite still, but for an occasional twitch of its tail.
column2: 一隻有斑點的白色雪豹趴在吉妮絲寢室的地板上，牠靜靜地趴著，只有尾巴偶爾抽動一下。

column1: Genesta's bed chamber is very beautiful and serene. Genesta lies very still upon the seashell bed; her concerned fairy helpers hovering near, and her pet snow leopard lying close by.
column2: 吉妮絲的寢室美麗而寧靜。吉妮絲靜靜地躺在貝殼床上，擔憂的小仙女們在一旁盤旋，她的寵物雪豹也伏在附近。

column1: You give the magic talisman to the dying fairy. Almost instantly, the color returns to her cheeks and her green eyes begin to sparkle. She sits up in bed, stretches, and smiles at you.
column2: 你把魔法護身符交給垂死的仙女。幾乎是瞬間，她的臉頰恢復了血色，綠色的雙眼開始閃閃發光。她從床上坐起身，伸了個懶腰，對你微笑。

column1: Not with Genesta in it!
column2: 吉妮絲還在裡面，不能這麼做！

column1: No. They belong to Genesta.
column2: 不行，這些是吉妮絲的東西。

column1: That belongs to Genesta.
column2: 那是吉妮絲的東西。

column1: As you attempt to speak to the gravely ill Genesta, her small fairy helpers flutter madly about, trying to prevent you from disturbing her.
column2: 你想跟病重的吉妮絲說話，她的小仙女們卻在四周瘋狂飛舞，想阻止你打擾她。

column1: THAT is DEFINITELY NOT the thing to do!
column2: 那絕對不是該做的事！

column1: You cannot get Genesta.
column2: 你不能把吉妮絲帶走。

column1: She needs her talisman, not a kiss!
column2: 她需要的是護身符，不是一個吻！

column1: You can start by helping to get her talisman back from Lolotte.
column2: 你可以先幫她從樂樂蒂手中奪回護身符。

column1: Genesta's constant companions are small fairies with fluttering wings and brightly-colored gowns. They utter not a word.
column2: 吉妮絲身邊總跟著幾個小仙女，振動著翅膀，穿著鮮豔的長袍，一句話也不說。

column1: It's impossible to obtain a fairy.
column2: 不可能抓到仙女。

column1: The little fairies do not need kissing.
column2: 小仙女們不需要親吻。

column1: The little fairies don't need your help; Genesta does.
column2: 小仙女們不需要你的幫助，吉妮絲才需要。

column1: Snow leopards do not talk!
column2: 雪豹不會說話！

column1: Oh, yeah. You're going to kill a big snow leopard with your bare hands!
column2: 喔，是嗎？你打算徒手殺死一隻大雪豹！

column1: Not this cat!
column2: 這隻貓可不行！

column1: That's probably not a good idea.
column2: 這恐怕不是個好主意。

column1: The snow leopard doesn't want it.
column2: 雪豹不想要這個。

column1: Genesta only needs her talisman.
column2: 吉妮絲只需要她的護身符。

column1: Genesta's tiny fairy helpers flit furiously about, trying to keep you from disturbing the dying fairy.
column2: 吉妮絲的小仙女們急速飛舞，想阻止你打擾這位垂死的仙女。

column1: You have saved my life, Rosella! the beautiful fairy says gratefully.
column2: 「你救了我一命，羅塞拉！」美麗的仙女感激地說。

column1: And you have saved Tamir from the persecution of Lolotte. We are deeply indebted to you.
column2: 「你也讓塔米亞免於樂樂蒂的迫害。我們都深深感激你。」

column1: I want to go outside; I need to feel the warm sun on my face!
column2: 「我想到外面去，我需要感受陽光灑在臉上的溫暖！」

column1: Exuberantly, she waves her magic wand and ...
column2: 她興高采烈地揮動魔杖，接著……

column1: Where do the stairs lead?
column2: 這樓梯通往哪裡？

column1: The stairs lead down to the entry hall.
column2: 樓梯向下通往入口大廳。

column1: From here, the tower stairs both go upward, and downward.
column2: 從這裡看，塔樓的階梯往上和往下都有。

column1: You're not close enough to the window.
column2: 你離窗戶不夠近。

column1: A beautifully embroidered tapestry hangs on the gleaming wall.
column2: 一幅繡工精美的掛毯掛在光亮的牆上。

column1: Genesta must love beautiful plants and flowers.
column2: 吉妮絲一定很喜歡美麗的植物和花卉。

column1: You are on a tower stairway of the island's ivory palace.
column2: 你站在這座島上象牙宮殿的塔樓階梯上。

column1: This is the entry hall of the enchanted ivory palace. Two of the doors are closed, and presumably, off-limits to uninvited visitors. The left doorway, however, is open.
column2: 這裡是魔法象牙宮殿的入口大廳。兩扇門都關著，想必是不許不速之客進入。不過左邊的門是開著的。

column1: Two of the doors are closed, locked, and off-limits to uninvited visitors; but, the left doorway is open. The main palace door is behind you.
column2: 兩扇門都關著且上了鎖，不許不速之客進入；不過左邊的門是開著的。宮殿的正門就在你身後。

column1: An unusual vine clings to the back wall of the palace entry hall.
column2: 一株奇特的藤蔓攀附在宮殿入口大廳的後牆上。

column1: You can't. The door is locked.
column2: 你辦不到，這扇門鎖著。

column1: You don't have the right key.
column2: 你沒有對的鑰匙。

column1: There's no answer.
column2: 沒有人回應。

column1: There is nothing within reach to use it on.
column2: 附近沒有東西可以用它。

column1: The large bed occupies most of the room.
column2: 一張大床佔據了房間大半空間。

column1: It must hold the ogre's and ogress' clothes.
column2: 這裡面應該放著食人魔和女食人魔的衣物。

column1: A bear rug lies on the floor by the bed.
column2: 床邊地板上鋪著一張熊皮地毯。

column1: You see the forest out the window.
column2: 你從窗外看見森林。

column1: The stairs lead downward.
column2: 樓梯向下通去。

column1: There is nothing of importance in the barrel.
column2: 木桶裡沒有什麼重要的東西。

column1: You look in the mirror and see the reflection of a poor, but beautiful, peasant girl.
column2: 你看向鏡子，看見一個貧窮卻美麗的農家女孩的倒影。

column1: The door is closed. From behind it, you hear a soft clucking sound.
column2: 門是關著的，你聽見門後傳來輕輕的咯咯聲。

column1: You see a solid, wooden door.
column2: 你看見一扇厚實的木門。

column1: You have entered the ogres' upstairs bedroom where a huge bed dominates the place. In the left wall, you notice a closed door. %s
column2: 你進入了食人魔夫婦樓上的臥室，一張巨大的床佔據了整個房間。你注意到左牆上有一扇關著的門。%s

column1: The windows don't open.
column2: 窗戶打不開。

column1: You knock on the door, but receive no answer. You hear a soft, clucking from behind the door.
column2: 你敲了敲門，卻沒有人回應。你聽見門後傳來輕輕的咯咯聲。

column1: You don't want to mess with this ogress. You'd better get out of here!
column2: 你可不想招惹這個女食人魔，最好快點離開這裡！

column1: You have no way of killing a giant ogress!
column2: 你沒有辦法殺死一個巨大的女食人魔！

column1: You don't want to get that close to the ogress!
column2: 你可不想靠女食人魔那麼近！

column1: Poor Rosella! Looks like you'll join the deer in the stew pot tonight.
column2: 可憐的羅塞拉！看來你今晚要跟那隻鹿一起下鍋燉了。

column1: The terrible ogre is asleep at the table, snoring loudly.
column2: 可怕的食人魔在桌邊睡著了，鼾聲大作。

column1: It's a large wooden table.
column2: 這是一張大木桌。

column1: There are two chairs at the table.
column2: 桌邊有兩張椅子。

column1: A worn rug lies on the floor.
column2: 地板上鋪著一張破舊的地毯。

column1: The stairway goes upwards.
column2: 樓梯往上通去。

column1: The front door leads outside. There is an open doorway to the right, and a closet door under the stairs.
column2: 前門通往外面。右邊有一扇開著的門，樓梯下方還有一扇儲藏室的門。

column1: This ogre looks MEAN! Better get out of there...FAST!
column2: 這個食人魔看起來很兇惡！最好快點離開……動作要快！

column1: You don't see him now.
column2: 你現在沒看見他。

column1: The big dog is totally engrossed in its bone.
column2: 這隻大狗完全沉浸在啃骨頭裡。

column1: This is definitely not your basic cute pooch. This dog means business.
column2: 這絕對不是普通的可愛小狗，這隻狗可不是好惹的。

column1: This isn't the most cozy-looking living room you've ever seen. In fact, the ogres' house looks almost...frightening!
column2: 這間客廳看起來一點也不溫馨。事實上，食人魔的家幾乎……令人毛骨悚然！

column1: That's not a good idea!
column2: 這可不是個好主意！

column1: That won't help you, Rosella!
column2: 這對你沒有幫助，羅塞拉！

column1: The dog is too interested in its bone to listen to you.
column2: 這隻狗只顧著啃骨頭，根本不理你。

column1: Soothing words will not placate this dog.
column2: 好言好語安撫不了這隻狗。

column1: You couldn't kill that big dog.
column2: 你殺不死這隻大狗。

column1: Not this dog!
column2: 這隻狗可不行！

column1: This dog will not be gentled.
column2: 這隻狗沒辦法被馴服。

column1: You woudln't want this dog, even if you COULD get it.
column2: 就算你抓得到，也不會想要這隻狗。

column1: You foolishly speak to the sleeping ogre.
column2: 你不智地對熟睡的食人魔說話。

column1: You're too far away for him to hear you.
column2: 你離得太遠，他聽不到你說話。

column1: This is no time for conversation.
column2: 現在不是聊天的時候。

column1: Just whom are you talking to?
column2: 你到底在跟誰說話？

column1: It is open.
column2: 這是開著的。

column1: It is already open.
column2: 這已經是開著的了。

column1: You're not close enough to any doors.
column2: 你離任何一扇門都不夠近。

column1: It is closed.
column2: 這是關著的。

column1: Do you see one here?
column2: 你在這裡看見一個了嗎？

column1: You have no way of killing a giant ogre!
column2: 你沒有辦法殺死一個巨大的食人魔！

column1: He wouldn't like it, and neither would you!
column2: 他不會喜歡的，你也不會喜歡！

column1: It's too late for that, Rosella!
column2: 已經太遲了，羅塞拉！

column1: Sorry, you don't have it to throw!
column2: 抱歉，你沒有東西可以丟！

column1: That's REALLY not a good idea!
column2: 這真的不是個好主意！

column1: What bone?
column2: 什麼骨頭？

column1: That's ridiculous.
column2: 這太荒謬了。

column1: You don't want to get that close to the ogre.
column2: 你可不想靠食人魔那麼近。

column1: The dog would not be interested in it.
column2: 這隻狗對這個沒興趣。

column1: Got ya!
column2: 「抓到你了！」

column1: You see the ogre sitting at the table.
column2: 你看見食人魔坐在桌邊。

column1: You see the ogre sleeping at the table. You had better be real quiet...
column2: 你看見食人魔在桌邊睡著了，你最好放輕腳步……

column1: SQUAWK! SQUAWK! Oh, oh! The hen has woken the ogre!
column2: 「咯咯！咯咯！」糟了，母雞把食人魔吵醒了！

column1: With a grunt, the ogre suddenly wakes up! Noticing you, he quickly jumps from his chair and heads your way.
column2: 食人魔哼了一聲，突然醒了過來！他一看見你，立刻跳下椅子朝你衝來。

column1: Now you've gone and done it! The vicious ogre grabs you and drags you toward the kitchen. As to what happens in the kitchen, let's just say that you are cordially invited to BE dinner.
column2: 這下你闖大禍了！凶惡的食人魔一把抓住你，把你拖向廚房。至於廚房裡發生了什麼事，只能說——你被誠摯邀請成為今晚的晚餐。

column1: Poor Rosella! Looks like you'll join the deer in the stew pot tonight!!
column2: 可憐的羅塞拉！看來你今晚要跟那隻鹿一起下鍋燉了！！

column1: Watch out! A VERY unfriendly bulldog rushes toward you!
column2: 小心！一隻非常兇惡的鬥牛犬朝你衝了過來！

column1: You weren't fast enough, Rosella! You were nothing but a bite-sized morsel for that big dog!
column2: 你的動作不夠快，羅塞拉！你只不過成了那隻大狗的一口點心！

column1: Quickly, you toss the bone to the big dog who catches it in mid-air. Happy with his surprise gift, he takes it to his favorite rug, lies down, and begins to gnaw at it. He seems to have completely forgotten you.
column2: 你迅速把骨頭丟給大狗，牠在半空中接住了。牠對這份意外的禮物十分滿意，叼著骨頭走到牠最喜歡的地毯上，趴下來啃了起來，似乎已經完全忘了你的存在。

column1: That is the carving table.
column2: 那是切肉用的桌子。

column1: Something TERRIBLE is cooking on the stove! You have NO desire to know what it is!
column2: 爐子上正煮著什麼可怕的東西！你一點也不想知道那是什麼！

column1: Whatever's in the pot smells AWFUL!
column2: 鍋裡不管煮的是什麼，聞起來都糟透了！

column1: You smell something HORRIBLE cooking on the stove in the ogre's kitchen! As a matter of fact, you get the feeling that this is NOT a safe place to be!
column2: 你聞到食人魔廚房的爐子上正煮著什麼可怕的東西！事實上，你有種預感，這裡一點也不安全！

column1: Now you blew it! You foolishly spoke to the ogress, and drew her attention toward you. Now she's headed your way!
column2: 這下你搞砸了！你不智地對女食人魔說話，引起了她的注意。她現在朝你衝過來了！

column1: It's too late. You can't help the deer now.
column2: 太遲了，你現在幫不了那隻鹿了。

column1: You're wasting time, Rosella.
column2: 你在浪費時間，羅塞拉。

column1: Why not just look out the open door?
column2: 何不直接從開著的門往外看呢？

column1: There is a large keyhole under the doorknob of the closet door.
column2: 儲藏室門的門把下方有一個大鎖孔。

column1: There is nothing of importance on the floor.
column2: 地板上沒有什麼重要的東西。

column1: You have found yourself inside the ogres' small closet. For a closet, it's surprisingly empty. You notice a large keyhole under the doorknob of the closet door.
column2: 你發現自己身在食人魔家的小儲藏室裡。以儲藏室來說，這裡出奇地空曠。你注意到門把下方有一個大鎖孔。

column1: It's already open.
column2: 已經是開著的了。

column1: It's already closed.
column2: 已經是關著的了。

column1: The ogre roars, "Fe, fi, fo, fum!  I smell the blood of a..."
column2: 食人魔咆哮道：「咈！咦！咈！噗！我聞到了……的血腥味！」

column1: Quiet, you old goat! interrupts the ogress. "That's just a deer you smell! You know how you get when you're hungry. Here! Eat!"
column2: 「安靜，你這隻老山羊！」女食人魔打斷道。「你聞到的只是一隻鹿而已！你也知道自己餓的時候會變成什麼樣子。來，吃吧！」

column1: Wife! shouts the ogre. "Bring me my hen!"
column2: 「老婆！」食人魔喊道。「把我的母雞拿來！」

column1: Lay! orders the ogre.
column2: 「下蛋！」食人魔命令道。

column1: With excitement, you watch the hen lay a golden egg, which the ogre takes. He yawns, then falls asleep on the table. Soon, his snoring rattles the walls.
column2: 你興奮地看著母雞下出一顆金蛋，食人魔一把拿走。牠打了個哈欠，隨即在桌上睡著了。不久，牠的鼾聲震得牆壁作響。

column1: You hear the ogre leaving.
column2: 你聽見食人魔離開的聲音。

column1: He's still there...
column2: 牠還在那裡……

column1: The house is nice and clean now.
column2: 這間屋子現在整潔乾淨。

column1: There are seven little beds.
column2: 這裡有七張小床。

column1: The narrow stairs lead downward.
column2: 狹窄的樓梯往下延伸。

column1: There is nothing under the beds.
column2: 床底下什麼都沒有。

column1: The dwarfs share one chest of drawers.
column2: 小矮人們共用一個五斗櫃。

column1: Quit being a snoop, Rosella!
column2: 別再東翻西找了，羅塞拉！

column1: A nice braided rug lies on the floor.
column2: 地上鋪著一張漂亮的編織地毯。

column1: You have entered the small upstairs bedroom of the Seven Dwarfs. %s
column2: 你走進了七個小矮人樓上的小臥室。%s

column1: The house needs more than that!
column2: 這間屋子需要的可不只這樣！

column1: You can't. It's too full of dirty, messy, dwarf clothes.
column2: 你辦不到。裡面塞滿了小矮人又髒又亂的衣服。

column1: You have better things to do.
column2: 你還有更重要的事要做。

column1: Oh, oh! You've been caught trespassing! You are unceremoniously ordered out by an angry dwarf.
column2: 糟了！你被抓到擅自闖入！一個生氣的小矮人毫不客氣地把你趕了出去。

column1: The clock is broken.
column2: 這座鐘壞了。

column1: There is nothing to interest you in the cupboards.
column2: 櫥櫃裡沒有什麼引起你興趣的東西。

column1: This house needs more than that!
column2: 這間屋子需要的可不只這樣！

column1: It is already closed.
column2: 它已經關上了。

column1: Not while you're in here.
column2: 你人還在這裡，不行。

column1: Twice was enough.
column2: 兩次就夠了。

column1: The room is nice and clean now.
column2: 這間房間現在整潔乾淨。

column1: Haven't you had enough already!!
column2: 你還沒做夠嗎！！

column1: You don't want dirty dishes!
column2: 你才不想要髒碗盤！

column1: You don't need them.
column2: 你不需要這些東西。

column1: You don't need the broom.
column2: 你不需要這把掃帚。

column1: What broom?
column2: 什麼掃帚？

column1: The pot doesn't belong to you.
column2: 這口鍋子不是你的。

column1: You don't need the soup bowls.
column2: 你不需要這些湯碗。

column1: You're not hungry now.
column2: 你現在並不餓。

column1: The soup doesn't belong to you.
column2: 這鍋湯不是你的。

column1: What pouch?!
column2: 什麼小袋子？！

column1: The soup is delicious!
column2: 這湯真好喝！

column1: There is a blue pouch in the center of the table.
column2: 桌子中央放著一個藍色小袋子。

column1: You can see the forest out the window.
column2: 你能從窗外看見森林。

column1: Clean soup bowls are set on the mantel above the fireplace.
column2: 乾淨的湯碗擺在壁爐上方的壁爐架上。

column1: There is nothing on top of the mantel.
column2: 壁爐架上什麼都沒有。

column1: You see a long, wooden table.
column2: 你看見一張長長的木桌。

column1: You also notice a blue pouch.
column2: 你還注意到一個藍色小袋子。

column1: You see a long, wooden table. Empty soup bowls are left on it.
column2: 你看見一張長長的木桌，上面留著幾個空湯碗。

column1: The narrow stairs lead up to the bedroom.
column2: 狹窄的樓梯通往樓上的臥室。

column1: You see many cupboards.
column2: 你看見許多櫥櫃。

column1: Clean soup bowls are set on the mantle above the fireplace.
column2: 乾淨的湯碗擺在壁爐上方的壁爐架上。

column1: Empty soup bowls are left on the table.
column2: 空湯碗留在桌上。

column1: You see a pot of soup bubbling over a cheery fire.
column2: 你看見一鍋湯在歡快的爐火上咕嘟冒泡。

column1: A cheery fire heats a bubbling pot of soup in the fireplace.
column2: 壁爐裡歡快的爐火正煮著一鍋咕嘟冒泡的湯。

column1: A rag rug covers the floor.
column2: 地上鋪著一張布條地毯。

column1: The sink is clean and empty.
column2: 水槽乾淨又空蕩。

column1: There are dirty dishes in the sink.
column2: 水槽裡有髒碗盤。

column1: The dishes are dirty.
column2: 這些碗盤是髒的。

column1: The dishes have been washed and put away.
column2: 碗盤已經洗好收起來了。

column1: This clock has seen better days. The cuckoo has flown away.
column2: 這座鐘已經風光不再，布穀鳥也早已飛走了。

column1: This clock is broken.
column2: 這座鐘壞了。

column1: The closet contains only an old broom.
column2: 這個櫃子裡只有一把舊掃帚。

column1: The closet is closed.
column2: 這個櫃子關著。

column1: There's nothing much in the closet except for an old broom.
column2: 這個櫃子裡除了一把舊掃帚，沒什麼別的東西。

column1: You see a closet door.
column2: 你看見一扇櫃子的門。

column1: You see an old broom.
column2: 你看見一把舊掃帚。

column1: You can't see any brooms from here.
column2: 你從這裡看不到任何掃帚。

column1: This is the main room of the Seven Dwarfs' cozy tree house.
column2: 這裡是七個小矮人溫馨樹屋的主廳。

column1: What a mess it is!
column2: 這裡真是亂七八糟！

column1: Phew! I hope that's the last of it!
column2: 「呼！希望這是最後一次了！」

column1: It is a ramshackle wooden door.
column2: 這是一扇搖搖欲墜的木門。

column1: You look out the open door of the diamond mine, but notice nothing.
column2: 你望向鑽石礦坑敞開的門外，卻沒發現什麼。

column1: Diamonds glitter and sparkle from the earthen walls of the Seven Dwarfs' diamond mine. Within it, you see the little men busily at work.
column2: 七個小矮人的鑽石礦坑，泥土牆上鑽石閃閃發光。礦坑裡，你看見矮小的男人們正忙著工作。

column1: You have encountered a group of small men working in this diamond mine. They're not too friendly, though, as one of them forcefully tells you to leave the premises!
column2: 你遇上了一群在這座鑽石礦坑裡工作的矮小男人。他們不怎麼友善，其中一個粗聲粗氣地叫你離開這裡！

column1: The dwarf is not interested.
column2: 這個小矮人沒有興趣。

column1: You see the busy little dwarfs hard at work within the diamond mine. The diamonds flash and sparkle from the earthen walls.
column2: 你看見忙碌的小矮人們在鑽石礦坑裡辛勤工作，泥土牆上鑽石閃爍生輝。

column1: One dwarf appears to be idling by the diamond bucket.
column2: 有個小矮人似乎正在鑽石桶旁閒晃。

column1: You see a bucket brim full of sparkling diamonds.
column2: 你看見一個裝滿閃亮鑽石的桶子，滿到桶口。

column1: You attempt to talk with the dwarf by the bucket again, but he only ignores you. You get the message.
column2: 你再次試著跟桶子旁的小矮人說話，但他只是不理你。你懂了。

column1: You attempt to kiss the dwarf, but he seems embarrassed by it.
column2: 你試著親吻這個小矮人，但他似乎因此感到很尷尬。

column1: The bucket is not yours, Rosella!
column2: 這個桶子不是你的，羅塞拉！

column1: You were raised better than that!
column2: 你的家教可不是這樣的！

column1: You say "hello" to the small man next to the diamond bucket. He seems amazed to see you here. "Rosella!" he exclaims. "What're you doing here? Don't you know this ain't no place for a fine girl like yourself?" You don't agree with that, but you don't argue.
column2: 你向鑽石桶旁的矮小男人打了聲招呼。他看見你在這裡似乎很驚訝。「羅塞拉！」他驚呼道。「妳在這裡做什麼？妳不知道這種地方不適合像妳這樣的好姑娘嗎？」你不太認同這種說法，但沒有爭辯。

column1: Again, you speak to the dwarf by the diamond bucket. He peers up at you with mild agitation. "This ain't no place for a lady, Rosella!" he firmly states. "I don't wanna see you get hurt in here!"
column2: 你再次跟鑽石桶旁的小矮人說話。他有些不安地抬頭看著你。「這種地方不適合淑女，羅塞拉！」他語氣堅定地說。「我可不想看妳在這裡受傷！」

column1: When you try speaking to the small man again, he only mutters under his breath in perturbation.
column2: 你再試著跟這矮小男人說話，他只是心煩意亂地低聲嘀咕。

column1: Being an honest person, you offer the forgotten pouch of diamonds to the dwarf. His gruff exterior softens a bit. "Nah, you can keep it," he says. "We got plenty here. We also got an extra lantern we ain't using. Here, go ahead and take it." The dwarf's gruffness returns as he says, "Now, skedaddle on out of here!"
column2: 身為一個誠實的人，你把那袋被遺忘的鑽石交還給小矮人。他粗獷的外表稍稍軟化了些。「不用啦，妳留著吧，」他說。「我們這裡多得是。我們還有一盞用不到的多餘燈籠，來，妳拿去吧。」小矮人恢復了粗聲粗氣的樣子，說道：「好了，快從這裡滾出去吧！」

column1: Ouch!
column2: 哎唷！

column1: Watch out!
column2: 當心!

column1: Too late.
column2: 太遲了。

column1: The blind, one-eyed hags peer at you through a glass eye.
column2: 那些失明的獨眼老太婆透過一顆玻璃眼盯著你看。

column1: Three, old one-eyed crones inhabit this dreary cave. They possess a glass eye, which they pass from one to another, that enables them to see. Through the glass eye, they each in turn peer at you with a frightening mixture of curiosity and malice.
column2: 這座陰森的洞穴裡住著三個獨眼老太婆。她們共有一顆玻璃眼，輪流傳遞使用才能視物。透過這顆玻璃眼，她們輪流盯著你，眼神混雜著令人害怕的好奇與惡意。

column1: The old one-eyed witches seem powerless without their glass eye. You actually feel rather sorry for them.
column2: 這些獨眼老巫婆沒了玻璃眼似乎毫無辦法。你甚至有點同情她們。

column1: The ugly old hags are relieved to receive their precious glass eye.
column2: 這些醜陋的老太婆拿回她們珍貴的玻璃眼，鬆了一口氣。

column1: What scarab?
column2: 什麼聖甲蟲？

column1: You just manage to snatch the glass eye from the grasp of the old witches! Now they are totally blind and helpless.
column2: 你好不容易從老巫婆手中搶下那顆玻璃眼！現在她們完全失明、無助不已。

column1: Do you really think the witches will let you take their precious glass eye again?
column2: 你真的以為巫婆們會再讓你拿走她們珍貴的玻璃眼嗎？

column1: You toss the glass eye to the pleading witches. "Aha! Foolish girl!" they shriek. "Leave us be, or you'll flavor our brew!"
column2: 你把玻璃眼丟給苦苦哀求的巫婆們。「哈哈！傻姑娘！」她們尖叫道。「別再煩我們，不然妳就等著給我們的藥湯調味吧！」

column1: You throw the glass eye to the three old hags, who once again take turns peering at you. "Come closer, dear," one says. "We'd like to give a little gift to you."
column2: 你把玻璃眼丟給三個老太婆，她們再度輪流盯著你看。「靠近一點，親愛的，」其中一個說。「我們想送妳一份小禮物。」

column1: You couldn't do it.
column2: 你辦不到。

column1: You wouldn't want them.
column2: 你不會想要這些的。

column1: YUCK!!
The witches don't want it.
column2: 巫婆們不想要這個。

column1: You don't want them.
column2: 你不想要這些。

column1: This is a dismal and dreary cave. %s In the center of the cave, over a hot fire, boils a foul-smelling brew inside a large black cauldron. %s
column2: 這是一座陰暗淒涼的洞穴。%s 洞穴中央，熊熊烈火上，一口大黑鍋裡煮著散發惡臭的藥湯。%s

column1: A greenish, foul brew boils ominously within the cauldron.
column2: 一鍋泛綠的惡臭藥湯在鍋裡不祥地翻滾著。

column1: The green brew looks disgusting...and smells that way, too!
column2: 這鍋綠色藥湯看起來很噁心……聞起來也一樣！

column1: A hot fire burns under a large cauldron filled with bubbling brew.
column2: 熊熊烈火在裝滿咕嘟冒泡藥湯的大鍋下燃燒著。

column1: Outside the cave, you see the terrible grabbing trees.
column2: 洞穴外，你看見那些可怕的攫人樹。

column1: You see a black scarab on the cave floor.
column2: 你看見洞穴地上有一隻黑色聖甲蟲。

column1: There is nothing of interest on the cave floor.
column2: 洞穴地上沒有什麼值得注意的東西。

column1: There is nothing of interest on the rough cave walls.
column2: 粗糙的洞穴牆壁上沒有什麼值得注意的東西。

column1: You see bottles and jars of disgusting-looking stuff. None of it interests you, though.
column2: 你看見一堆瓶瓶罐罐，裝著看起來很噁心的東西。不過沒有一樣引起你的興趣。

column1: You shiver at the sight of a human skull on the shelf.
column2: 看見架上的人類頭骨，你不禁打了個寒顫。

column1: You don't want any of them.
column2: 你一樣都不想要。

column1: You wouldn't want it!
column2: 你才不會想要！

column1: You gag at the thought!
column2: 光是想到就讓你反胃！

column1: It's too heavy to carry.
column2: 太重了，拿不動。

column1: You knew you needed a hot bath, but not THIS hot!
column2: 你知道自己需要泡個熱水澡，但可沒想要熱成這樣！

column1: Give us back our eye! the old witches plead."
column2: 「把我們的眼睛還來！」老巫婆們哀求道。」

column1: Even though the old hags are frightening, you decide to speak to them. They don't answer as they are mumbling and muttering among themselves, while peering through their glass eye at you.
column2: 儘管這些老太婆讓人害怕，你還是決定跟她們說話。她們沒有回應，只是自顧自地喃喃低語，同時透過玻璃眼盯著你看。

column1: Again, you attempt to talk to the three one-eyed witches. This time, one cackles, "What a pretty little thing you are, dearie. Come here, and let me touch your golden hair."
column2: 你再次試著跟三個獨眼巫婆說話。這次，其中一個咯咯笑道：「妳真是個漂亮的小東西呢，親愛的。過來，讓我摸摸妳的金髮。」

column1: As you speak with them again, they beg you to come closer so they can see you better and feel your pretty hair.
column2: 你再次跟她們說話，她們懇求你靠近一點，好讓她們看清楚妳，摸摸妳漂亮的頭髮。

column1: Give us back our eye! the old witches plead.
column2: 「把我們的眼睛還來！」老巫婆們哀求道。

column1: Your voice is drowned out by the wails of the three ugly hags as they beg for the return of their eye.
column2: 你的聲音被三個醜老太婆的哭喊聲淹沒，她們正哀求你歸還她們的眼睛。

column1: We need our eye! the three crones cry. "Give it back to us!"
column2: 「我們需要那顆眼睛！」三個老太婆哭喊道。「快還給我們！」

column1: You again talk to the old witches, who cry and wail for the return of their glass eye.
column2: 你再次跟老巫婆們說話，她們哭哭啼啼地哀求你歸還她們的玻璃眼。

column1: You speak with the three ugly witches. They mutter to themselves, after which one cajoles, "Come here, girl. We have something to give you for being such a nice girl and returning our eye."
column2: 你跟三個醜巫婆說話。她們先是自言自語，接著其中一個哄勸道：「過來，姑娘。妳這麼乖，還把眼睛還給我們，我們有東西要送妳。」

column1: You try talking to the old hags again. In a syrupy voice, one hag says, "Don't be afraid, dear. Come here, we have a gift for you."
column2: 你再次試著跟老太婆們說話。其中一個用甜膩的聲音說：「別害怕，親愛的。過來，我們有份禮物要送妳。」

column1: Come over here, girl. The old, one-eyed witches attempt to entice you with promises of a gift, in payment for your return of their glass eye.
column2: 「過來這裡，姑娘。」老獨眼巫婆們試圖用禮物引誘你，作為你歸還玻璃眼的回報。

column1: As you re-enter the dismal cave, the three, one-eyed witches throw something toward you, which lands on the ground. "Here, take it," they cry. "It'll ward off the undead. You'll need it. Now, PLEASE give us back our eye!!"
column2: 你再次走進陰暗的洞穴，三個獨眼巫婆朝你丟來一樣東西，落在地上。「拿去吧，」她們喊道。「這能抵擋不死生物，妳會用得上的。現在，拜託把我們的眼睛還來！！」

column1: As you re-enter the dismal cave, the three, one-eyed witches plead, "Please! Give us back our eye!!"
column2: 你再次走進陰暗的洞穴，三個獨眼巫婆哀求道：「拜託！把我們的眼睛還來！！」

column1: You think about it.
column2: 你考慮了一下。

column1: A dusty, old pipe organ sits in the center of the tower room.
column2: 一台佈滿灰塵的古老管風琴，立在塔樓房間中央。

column1: The tower stairs spiral steeply downward.
column2: 塔樓的樓梯陡峭地螺旋向下延伸。

column1: There is nothing but dust behind the old organ.
column2: 這台舊管風琴後面除了灰塵什麼都沒有。

column1: You see nothing in the organ.
column2: 你在管風琴裡什麼都沒看見。

column1: Dust and cobwebs cover the old organ.
column2: 灰塵與蜘蛛網覆蓋著這台舊管風琴。

column1: There is a bench in front of the old organ.
column2: 舊管風琴前有一張長凳。

column1: You see a skeleton key in the drawer.
column2: 你看見抽屜裡有一把萬能鑰匙。

column1: You see an empty drawer.
column2: 你看見一個空抽屜。

column1: What drawer?
column2: 什麼抽屜？

column1: There's no need to move the bench.
column2: 沒必要移動這張椅凳。

column1: You find it difficult to read the old sheet music, but you attempt it anyway.
column2: 你覺得這份舊樂譜很難辨讀，但你還是試著讀了讀。

column1: What music?
column2: 什麼樂譜？

column1: You are not sitting at the organ.
column2: 你沒有坐在風琴前。

column1: You can only remember one "oldie, but goodie."
column2: 你只記得一首「陳年老調，但依舊動聽」的曲子。

column1: You're not sitting at the organ.
column2: 你沒有坐在風琴前。

column1: You are already.
column2: 你已經如此了。

column1: It would be better to move to the inside of the bench.
column2: 最好先移到椅凳裡面去。

column1: You are already standing.
column2: 你已經站著了。

column1: You take the skeleton key from the drawer.
column2: 你從抽屜裡拿出萬能鑰匙。

column1: What key?
column2: 什麼鑰匙？

column1: There's no need to close the drawer.
column2: 沒必要把抽屜關上。

column1: You don't see a drawer.
column2: 你沒看到抽屜。

column1: The drawer is already open.
column2: 抽屜已經開著了。

column1: The bench doesn't open.
column2: 這張椅凳打不開。

column1: You already found it.
column2: 你已經找到了。

column1: Drawer? What drawer?
column2: 抽屜？什麼抽屜？

column1: What a virtuoso you are, Rosella!
column2: 羅塞拉，你真是個演奏高手！

column1: What is this? A small drawer has opened in the organ!
column2: 這是什麼？風琴裡彈出了一個小抽屜！

column1: This old baby nursery must have once been a cheery place. Now, through neglect, cobwebs decorate the corners, wallpaper peels from the walls, and dust covers the nursery furniture.
column2: 這間老舊的嬰兒房想必曾經是個溫馨的地方。如今由於乏人照料，牆角結滿了蜘蛛網，壁紙從牆上剝落，塵埃覆蓋著房裡的家具。

column1: The chest of drawers is empty.
column2: 那個五斗櫃是空的。

column1: The chest of drawers looks old and neglected.
column2: 那個五斗櫃看起來又舊又乏人照料。

column1: You imagine the mother who must have rocked her baby in this rocking chair.
column2: 你想像著曾有位母親坐在這張搖椅上，輕輕搖著懷中的嬰兒。

column1: You see the cemetery out the window.
column2: 你從窗外看到了墓園。

column1: The baby cradle is empty.
column2: 那個搖籃是空的。

column1: With trepidation, you peek into the rocking cradle and see nothing. Tentatively you reach your hand into it. There is nothing there!!
column2: 你戰戰兢兢地探頭望進搖動的搖籃，什麼也沒看見。你試探性地伸手進去摸索。裡面什麼都沒有！！

column1: You see an empty cradle here.
column2: 你在這裡看到一個空搖籃。

column1: The baby cradle rocks to and fro as a baby's voice wails from within.
column2: 搖籃前後搖晃著，裡頭傳出嬰兒的哭聲。

column1: There is an old rocking horse here.
column2: 這裡有一匹老舊的搖搖馬。

column1: A worn pink rug adorns the floor.
column2: 地板上鋪著一張磨損的粉紅色地毯。

column1: That would waste your precious time.
column2: 那只會浪費你寶貴的時間。

column1: The cradle is already rocking!
column2: 搖籃已經在搖了！

column1: You're too big for it.
column2: 你的個頭太大了，坐不進去。

column1: You speak in a soft trembly voice to the fretful baby ghost. Unfortunately, the poor little thing continues its pathetic crying.
column2: 你用輕柔顫抖的聲音對著哭鬧不安的嬰兒幽靈說話。可惜這可憐的小東西仍舊淒楚地哭個不停。

column1: You'd like to, but you can't see it, or touch it.
column2: 你很想這麼做，但你既看不到它，也摸不到它。

column1: You reach your hand into the rocking cradle and feel around. There is nothing there but a strange coldness! A shiver runs down your spine as the unseen baby continues its incessant crying.
column2: 你伸手探進搖動的搖籃裡摸索。裡面什麼都沒有，只有一股詭異的寒意！一陣寒顫竄過你的背脊，而那看不見的嬰兒仍舊哭個不停。

column1: How can you kill something that is already dead?
column2: 一個早已死去的東西，你要怎麼殺它？

column1: You'd like to help the poor baby ghost, but how can you do that?
column2: 你很想幫幫這可憐的嬰兒幽靈，但要怎麼做才好呢？

column1: This baby doesn't eat!
column2: 這個嬰兒不吃東西！

column1: While trembling, you attempt to sing a lullaby to the whimpering baby ghost. It doesn't work. The poor thing continues its pitiful crying.
column2: 你顫抖著試著為哭泣的嬰兒幽靈唱一首搖籃曲。沒有用。這可憐的小東西仍舊悲切地哭著。

column1: Obviously, as the crying continues, the baby ghost isn't interested in it.
column2: 顯然，哭聲並未停止，這個嬰兒幽靈對此毫無興趣。

column1: You drop the silver rattle into the baby cradle. As you do so, the crying of the baby ghost and the rocking of the cradle cease. You breathe a big sigh of relief.
column2: 你把銀色搖鈴放進搖籃裡。就在此時，嬰兒幽靈的哭聲和搖籃的晃動都停止了。你鬆了一大口氣。

column1: The crying baby seems to be in here.
column2: 哭聲似乎是從這裡傳出來的。

column1: This old bedroom must have once been very nice. You could imagine a cheery fire in the fireplace, while its occupant slept snugly in the big bed. But sadly, this room has been abandoned for years.
column2: 這間老舊的臥室想必曾經十分雅致。你可以想像壁爐裡曾有溫暖的爐火，屋主也曾安穩地睡在那張大床上。但可惜這房間已經荒廢多年。

column1: You see the sad ghost of a beautiful young woman. She sits in her rocking chair and weeps as if her heart will break.
column2: 你看見一個美麗年輕女子的悲傷幽靈。她坐在搖椅上哭泣，彷彿心都要碎了。

column1: The old bed still looks comfortable.
column2: 這張舊床看起來依然舒適。

column1: The fireplace has been cold for years. There's nothing there.
column2: 這座壁爐已經冷了多年。裡面什麼都沒有。

column1: A worn rug lies beneath the bed.
column2: 床底下鋪著一張磨損的地毯。

column1: The lamp is old and dusty.
column2: 這盞燈又舊又滿是灰塵。

column1: A large, old chest of drawers rests against the wall.
column2: 一個又大又舊的五斗櫃靠牆放著。

column1: You see a ladder descending into the room from an open trapdoor in the ceiling.
column2: 你看見一道梯子從天花板上敞開的活板門垂降到房裡。

column1: You look up at the ceiling and notice a closed trapdoor.
column2: 你抬頭望向天花板，注意到一扇關閉的活板門。

column1: You look up through the open trapdoor, but can't see anything as it's too dark up there.
column2: 你抬頭望向敞開的活板門，但上面太暗了，什麼也看不見。

column1: The trapdoor is closed.
column2: 暗門關著。

column1: You look up through the open trapdoor, but can't make out anything as it's too dark up there.
column2: 你抬頭望向敞開的活板門，但上面太暗了，什麼也看不清。

column1: A ladder descends into the room from an open trapdoor in the ceiling.
column2: 一道梯子從天花板上敞開的活板門垂降到房裡。

column1: What ladder?
column2: 什麼梯子？

column1: You see a ladder, descending into the room from an open trapdoor in the ceiling.
column2: 你看見一道梯子，從天花板上敞開的活板門垂降到房裡。

column1: The ceiling trapdoor is closed.
column2: 天花板上的活板門是關著的。

column1: The trapdoor is already open!
column2: 活板門已經開著了！

column1: You have absolutely no way of reaching that trapdoor as it's too high. And moving the heavy furniture is out of the question. Forget it.
column2: 那扇活板門太高了，你根本無法搆到。而且搬動那些笨重的家具也不可行。算了吧。

column1: There is no reason to close it.
column2: 沒有理由把它關上。

column1: The trapdoor is already closed!
column2: 活板門已經關著了！

column1: Not from this side.
column2: 從這一側不行。

column1: You see nothing to climb.
column2: 你沒看到有什麼可以攀爬的東西。

column1: You can't climb that here.
column2: 你在這裡爬不上那個。

column1: This is too heavy to move.
column2: 這太重了，搬不動。

column1: You see the sad ghost of a beautiful young woman. She sits in her rocking chair and weeps as if her heart will break.
column2: 你看見一個美麗年輕女子的悲傷幽靈。她坐在搖椅上哭泣，彷彿心都要碎了。

column1: You try to comfort the lady ghost, but she doesn't respond. It seems as if she doesn't even know you're here as she continues her deep sobbing.
column2: 你試著安慰這位女士幽靈，但她毫無反應。她似乎完全不知道你在這裡，只是繼續深深地啜泣著。

column1: You can't get a ghost!
column2: 你抓不到幽靈！

column1: You can't catch a ghost!
column2: 你捉不住幽靈！

column1: How can you kill something that is already dead!
column2: 一個早已死去的東西，你要怎麼殺它！

column1: You can't, as the ghost has no substance.
column2: 你辦不到，因為幽靈沒有實體。

column1: You'd like to help the sad lady ghost, but how can you do that?
column2: 你很想幫幫這位悲傷的女士幽靈，但要怎麼做才好呢？

column1: You hand the lovely locket to the distraught ghost. The locket seems to make her very happy as she vanishes into thin air.
column2: 你把那個精美的墜子交給心煩意亂的幽靈。這個墜子似乎讓她非常開心，接著她便消失在空氣中。

column1: The lady ghost doesn't even acknowledge your presence.
column2: 這位女士幽靈根本沒注意到你的存在。

column1: The steps in this tower are steep and narrow. Be careful!
column2: 這座塔裡的階梯又陡又窄。小心點！

column1: Don't look down!
column2: 別往下看！

column1: You don't see anything of interest on the walls.
column2: 牆上沒有什麼引起你注意的東西。

column1: You're almost to the top!
column2: 你快到頂端了！

column1: The steps spiral steeply up the stone tower of this old house.
column2: 階梯沿著這棟老宅的石造塔樓陡峭地盤旋而上。

column1: Aaaaahhhhhhh!!
column2: 「啊啊啊啊啊啊啊！！」

column1: The old bed is massive and dominates the room.
column2: 這張老舊的床十分龐大，佔滿了整個房間。

column1: You don't see a window.
column2: 你沒看到窗戶。

column1: The drawers are empty.
column2: 這些抽屜是空的。

column1: You see a small dresser.
column2: 你看見一個小梳妝台。

column1: A chest of drawers, sporting a broken mirror, sets against the wall.
column2: 一個裝著破鏡子的五斗櫃靠牆放著。

column1: You look in the mirror and see the reflection of a poor, but beautiful, peasant girl.
column2: 你看向鏡子，看見一個貧窮卻美麗的農家女孩的倒影。

column1: You're not close enough to look in either mirror.
column2: 你離這兩面鏡子都不夠近，看不清楚。

column1: A tattered oriental rug covers the floor at the foot of the bed.
column2: 床腳鋪著一張破舊的東方地毯。

column1: It appears as if this was once the master bedroom. However, cobwebs hang everywhere, and dust covers the furniture.
column2: 這裡看起來曾經是主臥室。然而如今到處掛滿蜘蛛網，家具也覆滿了灰塵。

column1: There is nothing of interest in the old boxes.
column2: 這些舊箱子裡沒什麼值得注意的東西。

column1: It's just an old bird cage...nothing interesting there.
column2: 那只是個舊鳥籠……沒什麼有趣的。

column1: The top of the wooden chest is currently occupied by a naughty little ghost.
column2: 這個木箱上頭目前正坐著一個頑皮的小幽靈。

column1: The wooden chest is not open.
column2: 這個木箱沒有打開。

column1: You peek into the open chest, and find...an old set of sheet music! You take the sheet music and carry it with you.
column2: 你探頭望進敞開的箱子，發現……一份舊樂譜！你拿起樂譜，隨身帶著。

column1: You peek into the open chest, and find...nothing.
column2: 你探頭望進敞開的箱子，發現……什麼都沒有。

column1: An interesting wooden chest catches your attention.
column2: 一個有意思的木箱引起了你的注意。

column1: You can see into the room below. There's nothing there.
column2: 你可以看到下面的房間。裡面什麼都沒有。

column1: You can't see from here.
column2: 你從這裡看不到。

column1: The trapdoor in the attic floor is open. From it, a ladder leads to the room below.
column2: 閣樓地板上的活板門是開著的。一道梯子從那裡通往下面的房間。

column1: A ladder descends from the open trapdoor into the room below.
column2: 一道梯子從敞開的活板門垂降到下面的房間。

column1: The old junk is useless.
column2: 這些舊雜物沒有用。

column1: The little boy ghost laughs, and refuses to budge from his seat on top of the chest. It might help if he had a toy to play with.
column2: 這個小男孩幽靈笑了笑，賴在箱子上不肯挪動半步。如果他有個玩具可以玩，或許會有幫助。

column1: What ghost?
column2: 什麼幽靈？

column1: After adjusting your eyes to the gloom, you soon see that you have stumbled upon the musty attic of the old house. The trapdoor in the floor is open, and from it, a ladder leads down to the room below. Useless junk and boxes clutter the room. There is an interesting chest near the open trapdoor.
column2: 待你的眼睛適應了昏暗之後，很快就看出自己來到了這棟老宅裡霉味瀰漫的閣樓。地板上的活板門是開著的，一道梯子從那裡通往下面的房間。這裡堆滿了沒用的雜物和箱子。在敞開的活板門附近，有一個引人注意的箱子。

column1: There is no need to do that.
column2: 沒必要這麼做。

column1: The trapdoor is already open.
column2: 活板門已經開著了。

column1: The chest is too heavy. You cannot carry it.
column2: 這個箱子太重了，你搬不動。

column1: You're afraid to get too near the ghost.
column2: 你不敢太靠近這個幽靈。

column1: The chest is already closed.
column2: 箱子已經關上了。

column1: The chest is already open.
column2: 寶箱已經開著了。

column1: The boy ghost laughs and teases, and refuses to leave his spot on top of the chest.
column2: 這個男孩幽靈嘻笑戲弄著，不肯離開箱子上的位置。

column1: This old dining room has seen better days! The long table is littered with crumbs and dust, and against the wall, the empty hutch is covered with cobwebs.
column2: 這間老舊的餐廳也曾風光一時！長桌上散落著碎屑和灰塵，靠牆的空碗櫃則布滿了蜘蛛網。

column1: The dusty old table is littered with crumbs.
column2: 這張滿是灰塵的舊桌子上散落著碎屑。

column1: The chairs seem to be falling apart.
column2: 這些椅子看起來快要散架了。

column1: There is nothing of interest in the hutch.
column2: 這個碗櫃裡沒什麼值得注意的東西。

column1: A tarnished chandelier hangs above the dining table.
column2: 一盞失去光澤的吊燈懸掛在餐桌上方。

column1: You see crumbs here.
column2: 你在這裡看到一些碎屑。

column1: You have no use for crumbs.
column2: 碎屑對你沒有用。

column1: The old kitchen is quite bare. The fireplace has been cold for years, and the dusty pantry is empty but for old crumbs.
column2: 這間老舊的廚房相當空蕩。壁爐已經冷了多年，滿是灰塵的儲藏室裡除了陳年碎屑外空無一物。

column1: The pantry is bare.
column2: 這個儲藏室是空的。

column1: The fireplace is stone cold. An empty cooking pot sits in it.
column2: 壁爐早已冰冷。裡頭放著一個空鍋子。

column1: The cooking pot is empty and useless.
column2: 這個鍋子是空的，沒有用處。

column1: The old butterchurn is empty and useless.
column2: 這個舊奶油攪拌桶是空的，沒有用處。

column1: You're not near the window.
column2: 你離窗戶不夠近。

column1: The barrel is empty. You can't use it.
column2: 這個木桶是空的。你用不上它。

column1: There is nothing that interests you on the kitchen shelves.
column2: 廚房架子上沒有什麼引起你興趣的東西。

column1: An old ladder sits in the middle of the floor.
column2: 一道舊梯子擺在地板中央。

column1: The cooking pot is empty and useless. You don't need it.
column2: 這個鍋子是空的，沒有用處。你不需要它。

column1: The steps are narrow, steep, and spiral upwards.
column2: 這些階梯又窄又陡，盤旋向上。

column1: The secret door opens into the parlor.
column2: 這扇密門通往客廳。

column1: You see nothing of interest on the floor.
column2: 地板上沒有什麼特別的東西。

column1: You see nothing of interest on the walls.
column2: 牆上沒有什麼特別的東西。

column1: A torch burns on the wall.
column2: 牆上點著一支火把。

column1: The spiral stairs go way up!
column2: 螺旋階梯一路往上延伸！

column1: You have found a secret tower! Narrow steps spiral dizzingly upward. %s
column2: 你發現了一座秘密高塔！狹窄的階梯令人暈眩地盤旋而上。%s

column1: Just climb them.
column2: 爬上去就是了。

column1: What shovel?
column2: 什麼鏟子？

column1: The torch is firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 火把牢牢固定在牆上。

column1: You don't need to close it.
column2: 你不需要關上它。

column1: The secret door is already open.
column2: 密門已經是開著的了。

column1: Considering the disarray of the rest of the house, the parlor looks in relatively good order. However, the fireplace is cold and unused, the bookshelves are almost bare, and the old furnishings are dusty.
column2: 相較於這棟房子其他地方的凌亂，客廳看起來算是整齊的了。不過壁爐早已冷清未用，書架幾乎空空如也，老舊的家具也積滿了灰塵。

column1: You examine the few remaining books on the bookshelves. Only one catches your attention; it is entitled "The Compleat Works of William Shakespeare."
column2: 你查看書架上僅剩的幾本書。只有一本引起你的注意，書名是《威廉·莎士比亞全集》。

column1: You examine the few remaining books on the bookshelves. None interest you.
column2: 你查看書架上僅剩的幾本書，沒有一本引起你的興趣。

column1: You examine the books on this side of the room. None of these books are up the quality of reading you're used to...
column2: 你查看房間這一側的書，沒有一本夠得上你平常讀慣的水準……

column1: The portrait is firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這幅畫像牢牢掛在牆上。

column1: An interesting portrait of a young girl hangs above the fireplace. You gaze at it intently, and notice that her eyes seem to stare at the left wall of the parlor.
column2: 壁爐上方掛著一幅有趣的少女畫像。你仔細凝視，發現畫中少女的眼睛似乎正盯著客廳的左牆看。

column1: You see a secret door in the left wall.
column2: 你在左牆上看見一扇密門。

column1: You examine the left wall very closely and notice a little latch.
column2: 你仔細查看左牆，發現一個小門閂。

column1: You see nothing special on the wall.
column2: 牆上沒有什麼特別的東西。

column1: You see the doorway to the foyer.
column2: 你看見通往門廳的出入口。

column1: You see a portrait hanging above the fireplace.
column2: 你看見壁爐上方掛著一幅畫像。

column1: You don't see it now.
column2: 你現在看不到它。

column1: You could try flipping the little latch in the left wall.
column2: 你可以試著撥動左牆上的那個小門閂。

column1: From there, you can barely see the latch in the wall.
column2: 從那裡，你幾乎看不清牆上的門閂。

column1: You don't see a latch.
column2: 你沒看到門閂。

column1: The fireplace is empty.
column2: 壁爐裡空空如也。

column1: The fireplace hasn't been used in years.
column2: 這座壁爐已經好多年沒用過了。

column1: The old divan is still in pretty good shape; a bit dusty, though.
column2: 這張舊長沙發保存得還算不錯，只是有點積灰。

column1: You have discovered a secret door in the left wall!
column2: 你在左牆上發現了一扇密門！

column1: You see no special doors.
column2: 你沒看到什麼特別的門。

column1: There is nothing of interest in the dusty old cabinet.
column2: 這個滿是灰塵的舊櫃子裡沒有什麼特別的東西。

column1: The furniture is old and dusty.
column2: 這些家具又舊又積滿灰塵。

column1: An old oriental carpet lies on the floor.
column2: 地板上鋪著一張古老的東方地毯。

column1: There are no books here you need.
column2: 這裡沒有你需要的書。

column1: None of the other books interest you.
column2: 其他的書都引不起你的興趣。

column1: You remove the Shakespeare book from the shelf and carry it with you.
column2: 你把莎士比亞的書從架上取下，隨身帶著。

column1: You examine the few remaining books on the bookshelves. Only one catches your attention; it is entitled "The Compleat Works of William Shakespeare." You remove it from the shelf and carry it with you.
column2: 你查看書架上僅剩的幾本書。只有一本引起你的注意，書名是《威廉·莎士比亞全集》。你把它從架上取下，隨身帶著。

column1: You don't see a door to open.
column2: 你沒看到可以打開的門。

column1: You don't need to close the door.
column2: 你不需要關門。

column1: There is no door to close!
column2: 這裡沒有門可以關！

column1: You flip the latch in the wall--and behold!--you have discovered a secret door!
column2: 你撥動牆上的門閂——瞧！——你發現了一扇密門！

column1: This is the entry room of the old house. Downstairs, you see two open doorways, and upstairs, you see an additional two doorways. The room is cobwebbed and dusty, with wallpaper peeling from the walls.
