column2: 這是老宅的玄關。樓下你看見兩個敞開的出入口，樓上還有另外兩個出入口。房間裡結滿蜘蛛網、佈滿灰塵，牆紙也在牆上一片片剝落。

column1: The time is:   %u:%02u %s
column2: 時間是：   %u:%02u %s

column1: There is nothing behind the portraits.
column2: 畫像後面什麼都沒有。

column1: You notice two interesting portraits above the downstairs doorways. One, of a middle-aged woman, and the other, of a young man. You wonder who they are.
column2: 你注意到樓下出入口上方掛著兩幅有趣的畫像，一幅是位中年婦女，另一幅是位年輕男子。你好奇他們究竟是誰。

column1: You look in the broken mirror and see the reflection of a poor, but beautiful, peasant girl.
column2: 你望進那面破鏡子，看見鏡中映出一個貧窮卻美麗的農家女孩。

column1: The stairs lead up to the second story.
column2: 樓梯通往二樓。

column1: There is nothing of interest inside the grandfather clock.
column2: 這座落地鐘裡面沒有什麼特別的東西。

column1: An old grandfather clock sits in the corner. With each swing of its pendulum, time relentlessly marches on...
column2: 一座老舊的落地鐘立在角落。隨著鐘擺一次次擺動，時光無情地流逝……

column1: Doors lead in all directions.
column2: 四面八方都有門。

column1: A tattered rug lies on the floor.
column2: 地板上鋪著一張破舊的地毯。

column1: A once-beautiful, marble-topped table rests below the broken mirror.
column2: 破鏡下方擺著一張曾經華美的大理石面桌子。

column1: You can't. You don't know how.
column2: 你辦不到，你不知道該怎麼做。

column1: It's too heavy to move.
column2: 太重了，搬不動。

column1: The portraits are too high, you could never reach them. Besides, they're not important.
column2: 這些畫像掛得太高，你怎麼樣都碰不到，而且它們也不重要。

column1: What an incredible place! This looks like an ancient burial tomb, with hieroglyphics written on the walls, and an old coffin against the far wall. %s %s
column2: 好不可思議的地方！這裡看起來像座古老的墓穴，牆上刻滿象形文字，遠端牆邊還擺著一副古老的棺木。%s %s

column1: You cannot read the ancient hieroglyphics.
column2: 你看不懂這些古老的象形文字。

column1: The lid of the coffin is decorated with hieroglyphics.
column2: 棺蓋上裝飾著象形文字。

column1: You see a pile of rope on the end of the platform.
column2: 你看見平台一端堆著一堆繩索。

column1: A rope ladder extends from the platform down to the bottom of the crypt.
column2: 一條繩梯從平台垂到地窖底部。

column1: The platform towers over the floor of the crypt.
column2: 這座平台高高聳立在地窖地面之上。

column1: There are ancient hieroglyphics on the walls.
column2: 牆上刻著古老的象形文字。

column1: Pandora's Box is on the floor of the crypt.
column2: 潘朵拉的盒子就放在地窖的地面上。

column1: This mummy appears to be at least a thousand years old! Its' wrappings are loose and decayed...and it stinks!
column2: 這具木乃伊看起來至少有一千年歷史了！裹屍布鬆脫又腐爛……而且臭氣沖天！

column1: There is no mummy here.
column2: 這裡沒有木乃伊。

column1: There is no box here.
column2: 這裡沒有盒子。

column1: The rope is fine where it is.
column2: 繩索留在原處就好。

column1: You reach down and get the pile of rope. To your happy surprise, you find that the rope is actually a rope ladder attached to the end of the platform! Gladly, you throw the rope ladder over the edge of the platform, where it falls to the floor below.
column2: 你彎下腰撿起那堆繩索，驚喜地發現這其實是一條繩梯，一端還固定在平台上！你高興地把繩梯甩過平台邊緣，讓它垂落到下方的地面。

column1: Not from up here!
column2: 從這上面可不行！

column1: It's not here.
column2: 不在這裡。

column1: You can't do that here.
column2: 你在這裡不能這麼做。

column1: You'll have to get a little closer, Rosella.
column2: 你得再靠近一點才行，羅塞拉。

column1: It's already closed!
column2: 已經是關著的了！

column1: The coffin is much too heavy for you to move.
column2: 這副棺木太重了，你搬不動。

column1: You attempt a conversation with the old mummy, and receive nothing but a low moan in reply.
column2: 你試著跟這具老木乃伊說話，得到的回應只有一聲低沉的呻吟。

column1: There is no one here who you can talk to.
column2: 這裡沒有人可以讓你交談。

column1: Why would you want it?
column2: 你要那個做什麼？

column1: What a horrible thought!
column2: 這想法太可怕了！

column1: It's beyond help.
column2: 這已經沒救了。

column1: The mummy doesn't need anything.
column2: 這具木乃伊什麼都不需要。

column1: Look out for the mummy!!
column2: 小心木乃伊！！

column1: The mummy runs from your powerful scarab.
column2: 木乃伊被你手中強大的聖甲蟲嚇跑了。

column1: How did you get here without the scarab? You will be the next victim of the mummy!!
column2: 沒有聖甲蟲你怎麼到得了這裡？你將會是木乃伊的下一個犧牲者！！

column1: Boy, those guys are sometimes fast for their age!
column2: 哇，這些傢伙上了年紀動作居然還這麼快！

column1: Oops! You slipped.
column2: 哎呀！你滑倒了。

column1: Waterfalls sure look different when you're underneath them!
column2: 站在瀑布底下看，果然大不相同！

column1: The mountain, above the waterfall, rises steeply to the east.
column2: 瀑布上方的山勢朝東陡峭聳立。

column1: It sure is dark in there!
column2: 裡面可真是漆黑一片！

column1: You see rocks all around you.
column2: 你四周都是岩石。

column1: You see only moss growing on the rocks.
column2: 你只看見岩石上長著青苔。

column1: You see a board lying on the ground.
column2: 你看見地上躺著一塊木板。

column1: It's difficult to see much of anything through a waterfall.
column2: 隔著瀑布很難看清任何東西。

column1: What's this?! Why, it's a cave behind the waterfall! %s
column2: 這是什麼？！咦，瀑布後面竟然有個洞穴！%s

column1: The mountain is too steep to climb.
column2: 這座山太陡了，爬不上去。

column1: You don't like to climb rocks.
column2: 你不喜歡爬岩石。

column1: You take a drink of the cool river water. Mmmmm. Not too bad.
column2: 你喝了一口清涼的河水。嗯……味道還不錯。

column1: You're already in the water.
column2: 你已經在水裡了。

column1: It sure feels good to be yourself again!
column2: 能變回原本的自己，感覺真好！

column1: You kneel down and take a drink of the cool river water. Mmmmm. Not too bad.
column2: 你跪下來喝了一口清涼的河水。嗯……味道還不錯。

column1: Outside the cave, you see the waterfall.
column2: 洞穴外，你看見那道瀑布。

column1: You see a pile of bones by the cave entrance.
column2: 你看見洞口旁堆著一堆骨頭。

column1: This is the darkest cave you've ever seen! %s
column2: 這是你見過最黑暗的洞穴！%s

column1: By the light of the cave entrance, you can see a pile of bones.
column2: 藉著洞口透進來的光，你可以看見一堆骨頭。

column1: You don't need another one.
column2: 你不需要再一個了。

column1: After this dark cave, the outside world looks good.
column2: 走出這黑暗的洞穴後，外面的世界看起來真美好。

column1: Outside the cave, you see a large swamp.
column2: 洞穴外，你看見一片廣大的沼澤。

column1: Outside, the night sky is lit by a full moon.
column2: 外頭，夜空被一輪滿月照亮。

column1: Outside, the sky is very blue.
column2: 外頭，天空湛藍無比。

column1: This is the darkest cave you've ever seen!
column2: 這是你見過最黑暗的洞穴！

column1: You see an opening in the distance.
column2: 你看見遠處有個開口。

column1: It's too dark to see it.
column2: 太暗了，看不清楚。

column1: Where is it?!
column2: 它在哪裡？！

column1: It seems far away.
column2: 看起來還很遠。

column1: You don't have the board.
column2: 你沒有那塊木板。

column1: Oh, my goodness!!
column2: 「哦，我的天啊！！」

column1: A CHASM!!!
column2: 「是條深谷！！！」

column1: Feeling in the dark, you manage to place the board across the crevice. Now, can you cross it safely?
column2: 你在黑暗中摸索著，總算把木板架到裂縫上。現在，你能安全地通過嗎？

column1: Feeling in the dark again, you manage to pick up the board without dropping it into the crevice.
column2: 你再次在黑暗中摸索，成功把木板撿起來，沒讓它掉進裂縫裡。

column1: From here, a huge muddy swamp spreads eastward. Large moss-covered swamp trees tower overhead, while grass tufts and reedy plants poke up from the mire. You can hear the many chirpings and croakings of unseen swamp creatures.
column2: 從這裡望去，一片泥濘的大沼澤向東延伸。長滿青苔的沼澤巨樹高聳於頭頂，成叢的野草和蘆葦從泥沼中冒出。你可以聽見四周傳來看不見的沼澤生物此起彼落的鳴叫聲。

column1: A small cave leads into the mountain.
column2: 一個小洞穴通往山裡。

column1: Just Move.
column2: 走過去就是了。

column1: Not from here!
column2: 從這裡可不行！

column1: Just walk there.
column2: 走過去就是了。

column1: Just move there.
column2: 移動過去就是了。

column1: The snake is already mesmerized.
column2: 這條蛇已經被催眠了。

column1: How could you do that?
column2: 你怎麼能做那種事？

column1: It's not there.
column2: 不在那裡。

column1: The snake does not require anything from you.
column2: 這條蛇不需要你給任何東西。

column1: You can't give that.
column2: 你不能給那個。

column1: The snake would not be affected by that.
column2: 這條蛇不會受那個影響。

column1: The large cobra seems completely under your spell...but not for long!
column2: 大眼鏡蛇似乎完全被你的魔法迷住了……但不會太久！

column1: A HUGE cobra protects the small tree that bears the wondrous fruit; the fruit that your father's life depends upon.
column2: 一條巨大的眼鏡蛇守護著那棵結出神奇果實的小樹；那正是你父王性命所繫的果實。

column1: A HUGE cobra protects the small island.
column2: 一條巨大的眼鏡蛇守護著這座小島。

column1: The tree is small and scrawny. It looks like it's taken almost every ounce of its strength to produce this one, wondrous fruit.
column2: 這棵樹又小又瘦弱，看起來像是耗盡了幾乎全部的力氣，才結出這一顆神奇的果實。

column1: The small tree looks bare and lonely without its marvelous fruit. Oh, well; in a mere hundred years it'll have another one.
column2: 少了那顆神奇的果實，小樹顯得光禿又孤單。唉，沒關係，再過區區一百年，它就會再結一顆。

column1: You see a single glistening fruit upon the scrawny tree.
column2: 你看到那棵瘦弱的樹上，掛著一顆閃閃發亮的果實。

column1: A tiny tree grows on this little swamp island. Hanging from a small branch is a large, glistening fruit.
column2: 這座沼澤小島上長著一棵小樹。一根細枝上掛著一顆碩大、閃閃發亮的果實。

column1: A tiny tree grows on this little swamp island.
column2: 這座沼澤小島上長著一棵小樹。

column1: The grass tufts lead to the small island.
column2: 一叢叢的草通往這座小島。

column1: You see a small island in the middle of the huge swamp. Upon this island grows a scrawny little tree. %s Unfortunately, a huge cobra protects it.
column2: 你看到巨大沼澤中央有一座小島。島上長著一棵瘦弱的小樹。%s 可惜，有一條巨大的眼鏡蛇守護著它。

column1: Cobras don't usually talk.
column2: 眼鏡蛇通常不會說話。

column1: You have NO way of killing a big cobra.
column2: 你根本沒有辦法殺死這條大眼鏡蛇。

column1: You wouldn't want the cobra!
column2: 你才不會想要這條眼鏡蛇！

column1: That is NOT a good idea!
column2: 這可不是個好主意！

column1: You couldn't get close enough. Besides, the cobra's too big!
column2: 你沒辦法靠得夠近。而且，這條眼鏡蛇太大了！

column1: OH!! He gotcha! You die a painful death of snake poisoning.
column2: 哦！牠咬到你了！你痛苦地死於蛇毒。

column1: Nervously, you raise the flute to your mouth and begin to play a melodic tune to the big cobra, who begins to sway with the music. Soon, the snake is mesmerized by it, and falls into an hypnotic trance.
column2: 你緊張地把笛子舉到嘴邊，對著大眼鏡蛇吹奏起悠揚的曲調，牠開始隨著音樂搖擺。不久，這條蛇便被樂聲迷住，陷入催眠般的恍惚狀態。

column1: The treacherous path winds narrowly around the craggy mountain. In the distance, perched upon a rocky ledge, you see a dark, unfriendly castle.
column2: 這條危險的小徑沿著崎嶇的山勢蜿蜒而行。遠處一塊岩架上，聳立著一座陰森不友善的城堡。

column1: Lolotte's goons are not appealing at all! Besides having an unnatural pallor to their skin, they are creepy little creatures who fly upon wings that resemble those of bats.
column2: 樂樂蒂的爪牙一點也不討喜！牠們的皮膚有種不自然的蒼白，是一群靠著蝙蝠般翅膀飛行的詭異小生物。

column1: The mountain path is thin and dangerous. It seems to lead to the dark castle.
column2: 這條山路狹窄又危險，看起來通往那座陰森的城堡。

column1: The dark castle looks very forbidding. It perches precariously upon an over-hanging, mountain ledge. The path, upon which you are now treading, seems to lead directly to the sinister castle.
column2: 這座陰森的城堡看起來相當森然。它岌岌可危地立於一塊懸空的山岩上。你現在走的這條小路，似乎正通向那座陰森的城堡。

column1: The whole mountain is rocky.
column2: 整座山遍布岩石。

column1: The ground is a long way down.
column2: 地面在很遠的下方。

column1: You see a forest of trees below you.
column2: 你看到下方是一片樹林。

column1: The craggy mountains are much too steep to climb.
column2: 這片崎嶇的山勢太陡峭了，爬不上去。

column1: Lolotte's henchmen must not speak, as they only utter strange guttural sounds.
column2: 樂樂蒂的爪牙似乎不會說話，只發出奇怪的喉音。

column1: Why would you want one?!
column2: 你要那個做什麼？！

column1: The henchmen are not interested in your offering.
column2: 這些爪牙對你給的東西不感興趣。

column1: Oh, oh! Lolotte's goons!
column2: 糟了，糟了！樂樂蒂的爪牙！

column1: You are standing before Lolotte's dark castle on the edge of the rocky mountain. There is a small stable next to it.
column2: 你站在樂樂蒂那座陰森城堡前，城堡就在岩石山的邊緣。旁邊有一座小馬廄。

column1: Lolotte's sinister castle is situated on a rocky ledge of the steep craggy mountain. A small stable is located beside it.
column2: 樂樂蒂那座陰森的城堡，坐落在陡峭崎嶇山勢的一塊岩架上。旁邊有一座小馬廄。

column1: The crude thatch-roofed stable is located near the dark castle, on the edge of a cliff.
column2: 這座簡陋的茅草頂馬廄，位於陰森城堡附近的懸崖邊上。

column1: The mountains are rocky and steep. The castle perches precariously upon a large ledge.
column2: 山勢岩石密布又陡峭。城堡岌岌可危地立於一塊巨大的岩架上。

column1: The narrow path leads down the mountain.
column2: 這條狹窄的小路通往山下。

column1: The door has hideous carvings.
column2: 這扇門上刻著醜陋的雕飾。

column1: What's this?! Why, a beautiful red rose has been pushed under your door! You pick it up and carry it with you.
column2: 這是什麼？！竟然有人從門縫底下塞進來一朵美麗的紅玫瑰！你把它撿起來隨身帶著。

column1: Normally, this is Edgar's tower bedroom. Actually, for being in such an awful castle, it looks quite comfortable; but you're in no mood to care.
column2: 這裡平常是艾德加的塔樓臥室。老實說，以這座糟糕的城堡而言，這房間看起來還挺舒適的；但你現在沒心情在意這些。

column1: This is a most unusual, four-poster bed.
column2: 這是一張非常特別的四柱床。

column1: You see the mountain out the window.
column2: 你從窗外看見那座山。

column1: It's private.
column2: 這是私人的。

column1: You see a chest of drawers, with a mirror on top.
column2: 你看到一個抽屜櫃，上面放著一面鏡子。

column1: You see a beautiful rose lying by the door.
column2: 你看到門邊放著一朵美麗的玫瑰。

column1: A small rug lies on the floor by the bed.
column2: 床邊的地板上鋪著一小塊地毯。

column1: How can you even see the stairs from here?
column2: 你在這裡怎麼可能看得到樓梯？

column1: It's just a plain wooden door.
column2: 這只是一扇普通的木門。

column1: It is attached to the rose.
column2: 它附在那朵玫瑰上。

column1: The door is unlocked.
column2: 這扇門沒有上鎖。

column1: The skeleton key does not fit the lock.
column2: 萬能鑰匙插不進這個鎖。

column1: It already is.
column2: 已經是這樣了。

column1: Using the little gold key you quickly unlock the door.
column2: 你用那把小金鑰匙迅速打開了門鎖。

column1: How can you do that without a key?
column2: 沒有鑰匙你要怎麼做到？

column1: Use it for what?
column2: 用它來做什麼？

column1: Unlock it first.
column2: 先把鎖打開。

column1: It is already is open.
column2: 它已經是開著的了。

column1: You remove the little gold key from the red rose.
column2: 你從那朵紅玫瑰上取下了那把小金鑰匙。

column1: You see Edgar's bedroom door, and a winding stairway leading down the tower.
column2: 你看到艾德加的臥室門，還有一道蜿蜒而下通往塔樓底部的樓梯。

column1: The stone stairway leads down the tower.
column2: 這道石梯通往塔樓下方。

column1: It already is open.
column2: 它已經是開著的了。

column1: It already is unlocked.
column2: 它已經是打開鎖的了。

column1: You think to yourself, NOW WHAT!!
column2: 你心想：現在該怎麼辦！！

column1: You hear something at the door!
column2: 你聽到門外有動靜！

column1: It looks like you're destined to be...Mrs. Edgar!
column2: 看來你註定要成為……艾德加夫人了！

column1: The henchmen have taken your rose and you no longer have the gold key! It looks like you're destined to be...Mrs. Edgar!
column2: 爪牙們拿走了你的玫瑰，你已經沒有那把金鑰匙了！看來你註定要成為……艾德加夫人了！

column1: So, this is Lolotte's bed chamber. You question her taste in decorating as her furniture is a bit on the gaudy side. %s
column2: 原來這就是樂樂蒂的寢室。她的家具擺設有點俗豔，你不禁懷疑起她的品味。%s

column1: The bed is huge and takes up most of the room.
column2: 這張床巨大無比，幾乎佔滿了整個房間。

column1: A massive rug adorns the floor.
column2: 地板上鋪著一塊巨大的地毯。

column1: It's just a wooden door.
column2: 這只是一扇木門。

column1: The evil fairy sleeps soundly in her bed. She wears Genesta's magic talisman around her neck, and you see it gleaming upon her chest. As she sleeps, she makes very strange clicking sounds.
column2: 邪惡的仙女正在床上沉睡。她脖子上戴著吉妮絲的魔法護身符，你看見它在她胸前閃著光。她睡覺時發出十分奇怪的咯咯聲。

column1: Lolotte is quite dead now. %s
column2: 樂樂蒂現在已經徹底死了。%s

column1: Why would you want to?
column2: 你為什麼要這麼做？

column1: That would be a bad idea!
column2: 這可是個壞主意！

column1: She's dead now. Are you sure this is a good idea?
column2: 她現在已經死了。你確定這是個好主意嗎？

column1: Do you honestly think Lolotte needs anything from you?
column2: 你真的以為樂樂蒂會需要你的東西嗎？

column1: Waking the dead is a little more than even a princess can do!
column2: 就算貴為公主，也沒辦法把死人叫醒！

column1: You wouldn't want her.
column2: 你才不會想要她。

column1: Lolotte is unable to talk, as she is now dead.
column2: 樂樂蒂已經死了，沒辦法說話。

column1: You're already carrying it.
column2: 你已經帶著它了。

column1: You'll have to get a little closer.
column2: 你得再靠近一點才行。

column1: Not with Lolotte in it!!
column2: 樂樂蒂還在裡面呢，不行！！

column1: She is already dead.
column2: 她已經死了。

column1: It's tempting, but you don't have it in you.
column2: 雖然很誘人，但你下不了手。

column1: You have no way of doing that.
column2: 你沒有辦法做到這件事。

column1: Shoot what?
column2: 要射什麼？

column1: Sorry, girl, you have no more arrows!
column2: 抱歉了，姑娘，你的箭已經用完了！

column1: She already is dead.
column2: 她已經死了。

column1: She is dying.
column2: 她正在死去。

column1: You see Lolotte's bedroom door, and a winding stairway leading down the tower.
column2: 你看到樂樂蒂的臥室門，還有一道蜿蜒而下通往塔樓底部的樓梯。

column1: Which key do you want to use; the gold key, or the skeleton key?
column2: 你要用哪一把鑰匙：金鑰匙，還是萬能鑰匙？

column1: What do you want to do with the gold key?
column2: 你想用這把金鑰匙做什麼？

column1: What do you want to do with the skeleton key?
column2: 你想用這把萬能鑰匙做什麼？

column1: Lolotte sits straight up in bed, a look of agony upon her face. "What did you do to me?!" she shrieks. "I'm melting...melting!" No, wait! That's another story. Ahem! Let's try again.
column2: 樂樂蒂猛地從床上坐直，臉上滿是痛苦的神情。「你對我做了什麼？！」她尖叫著。「我要融化了……融化了！」不對，等等！那是另一個故事。呃哼！我們重來一次。

column1: It hurts! It hurts! Stop the pain! Stop it!
column2: 「好痛！好痛！住手，別再讓我痛了！住手！」

column1: You cringe as you watch the evil fairy die a painful death. What happened?, you wonder. How could Cupid's arrow have done that? You thought that those arrows were harmless, and contained only LOVE.
column2: 你不忍卒睹地看著邪惡的仙女痛苦死去。這是怎麼回事？你納悶著。丘比特之箭怎麼會有這種效果？你原以為那些箭無害，裡頭只裝著「愛」而已。

column1: Wait! That must be it! LOVE! Lolotte was so evil that when all that pure love entered her body, it acted as a poison to her system! In spite of yourself, you feel a sense of sweet revenge.
column2: 等等！一定是這樣！愛！樂樂蒂邪惡至極，那股純粹的愛一進入她的體內，就像毒藥一樣發作了！你不由自主地，感到一股甜美的復仇快感。

column1: As she breathes her last, Lolotte manages to gasp, "I don't know how...but I'll get you for this..."
column2: 樂樂蒂在嚥下最後一口氣之際，艱難地喘息道：「我不知道要怎麼做……但我一定會找你算這筆帳……」

column1: A bright, new day is dawning!
column2: 嶄新光明的一天，正在破曉！

column1: You aim the golden arrow toward Lolotte. You let go of the bow string. ZING! A direct hit!
column2: 你將金箭瞄準樂樂蒂，鬆開弓弦。咻！正中目標！

column1: You stupidly wake Lolotte up! "VIPER!" she screams, as she quickly sits up, points a gnarled finger at you, and zaps you into oblivion!
column2: 你愚蠢地把樂樂蒂吵醒了！「毒蛇之女！」她尖叫著，猛然坐起，用一根枯瘦的手指指向你，一道法力將你化為烏有！

column1: Edgar bows to you, acknowledging his evil mother's death. He begins to say, "You may now walk freely about the castle. I..." and suddenly begins to blush.
column2: 艾德加向你鞠躬，承認他邪惡的母親已經死了。他開口說：「妳現在可以自由在城堡裡走動了。我……」說著突然臉紅了起來。

column1: You have found yourself in a HORRIBLE cell! It looks as if others have found their way here, too. Hopefully, you won't end up like them!
column2: 你發現自己身處一間可怕的牢房！看來曾經也有其他人來過這裡。希望你不會落得跟他們一樣的下場！

column1: At least two unfortunate individuals have met their end here.
column2: 至少有兩名不幸的人，在這裡結束了性命。

column1: An ominous machine lurks in the corner of this cell. As to its purpose; you don't want to know!
column2: 一台不祥的機器潛伏在這間牢房的角落。至於它的用途——你不會想知道的！

column1: A whip hangs on the wall.
column2: 牆上掛著一條鞭子。

column1: The chains are securely attached to the wall.
column2: 這些鐵鍊牢牢固定在牆上。

column1: The window is too high.
column2: 這扇窗太高了。

column1: You don't know how to use this machine, and you don't EVER want to know!
column2: 你不知道該怎麼使用這台機器，而且你永遠都不想知道！

column1: The whip is securely attached to the wall. Besides, you wouldn't want it.
column2: 這條鞭子牢牢固定在牆上。再說，你也不會想要它。

column1: You can't. It's locked.
column2: 你辦不到，門鎖著呢。

column1: Try just walking through the doorway.
column2: 直接走過那道門試試看。

column1: It's not locked. In fact, it's still open!
column2: 它沒有上鎖，事實上還開著呢！

column1: No one is going to save you.
column2: 沒有人會來救你。

column1: You hear the henchmen returning. Perhaps, they are setting you free!
column2: 你聽到爪牙們回來了。說不定他們是要放你出去！

column1: The henchman motions you to follow him out of the cell...
column2: 那名爪牙示意你跟著他走出牢房……

column1: This seems to be some sort of storage room.
column2: 這裡似乎是某種儲藏室。

column1: And guess what's stored here... Yes, Pandora's Box and the magic hen!!
column2: 猜猜這裡存放著什麼……沒錯，是潘朵拉的盒子和魔法母雞！！

column1: And guess what's stored here... Yes, the magic hen!
column2: 猜猜這裡存放著什麼……沒錯，是魔法母雞！

column1: And guess what's stored here... Yes, Pandora's Box!
column2: 猜猜這裡存放著什麼……沒錯，是潘朵拉的盒子！

column1: What did you come in here for?
column2: 你進來這裡是要做什麼？

column1: There is nothing of interest in the boxes.
column2: 這些箱子裡沒有什麼有意思的東西。

column1: There is nothing of interest in the barrels.
column2: 這些木桶裡沒有什麼有意思的東西。

column1: There is nothing of interest in the chest.
column2: 這個箱子裡沒有什麼有意思的東西。

column1: There is nothing useful on the shelves.
column2: 架子上沒有什麼有用的東西。

column1: You don't need any bones now.
column2: 你現在不需要任何骨頭。

column1: The windows are too high.
column2: 這些窗戶太高了。

column1: There is nothing of interest on the walls.
column2: 牆上沒有什麼有意思的東西。

column1: The hen is wandering around the room.
column2: 那隻母雞正在房間裡四處遊蕩。

column1: There's no hen here.
column2: 這裡沒有母雞。

column1: These stone tower steps could be very treacherous!
column2: 這些塔樓的石階可能非常危險！

column1: There is no floor here; only stone steps.
column2: 這裡沒有地板，只有石階。

column1: The stone stairs spiral up the dark tower.
column2: 石階沿著陰森的塔樓螺旋而上。

column1: Watch your step on these narrow stairs!
column2: 這樓梯又窄又陡，小心腳步！

column1: This is the west end of a dim hallway. There is %s door here.
column2: 這是昏暗走廊的西端，這裡有%s門。

column1: It appears to be a cell door.
column2: 這似乎是一扇牢房的門。

column1: There is a candelabra on the table shedding dim candlelight.
column2: 桌上有一座燭台，散發著微弱的燭光。

column1: Candle light flickers from the candelabra.
column2: 燭台上的燭光搖曳不定。

column1: This is quite a fancy chair.
column2: 這是張相當華麗的椅子。

column1: There's nobody there...
column2: 那裡沒有人……

column1: It already is closed.
column2: 它已經關上了。

column1: Why don't you just open it?
column2: 你何不直接打開它呢？

column1: It's not for you.
column2: 這不是給你的。

column1: This is the east end of the hallway.
column2: 這裡是走廊的東端。

column1: It's a plain wooden door.
column2: 這是一扇樸素的木門。

column1: There is a candelabra on the table, from which dim candle light sheds.
column2: 桌上有一座燭台，散發出微弱的燭光。

column1: Lolotte's henchmen must not speak, as you only hear them utter guttural sounds.
column2: 樂樂蒂的手下似乎不會說話，你只聽見他們發出粗啞的咕噥聲。

column1: That's an AWFUL thought!
column2: 這想法真是太可怕了！

column1: There is no floor here, only stone steps.
column2: 這裡沒有地板，只有石階。

column1: There is an open doorway before you.
column2: 你面前有一扇敞開的門。

column1: You have discovered the castle's kitchen. Not a very pleasant kitchen, but a kitchen nonetheless. Against the back wall, two cabinets flank a cold stone fireplace. You see a long table under the one small window.
column2: 你發現了城堡的廚房，雖然稱不上舒適，但畢竟是間廚房。後牆邊，兩個櫥櫃夾著一座冰冷的石造壁爐，一張長桌擺在唯一的小窗下方。

column1: Here are all of your possessions!
column2: 這是你的所有物品！

column1: The cabinet is empty.
column2: 櫥櫃是空的。

column1: The cabinet is full of dishes.
column2: 櫥櫃裡裝滿了碗盤。

column1: You see two cabinets; one on each side of the stone fireplace.
column2: 你看到兩個櫥櫃，分別位於石造壁爐的兩側。

column1: There's nothing under the table.
column2: 桌子底下什麼都沒有。

column1: Nothing on the table interests you.
column2: 桌上沒有什麼引起你的興趣。

column1: The fireplace is unlit and cold. An empty iron pot sits in it.
column2: 壁爐沒有生火，冷冰冰的，裡面放著一口空鐵鍋。

column1: The iron pot in the fireplace is empty.
column2: 壁爐裡的鐵鍋是空的。

column1: There is nothing of interest in the barrel.
column2: 木桶裡沒有什麼引起你的興趣。

column1: You see the mountain out the window.
column2: 你從窗外看見那座山。

column1: You see an open doorway before you.
column2: 你看到面前有一扇敞開的門。

column1: There is nothing in the barrel.
column2: 木桶裡什麼都沒有。

column1: Here are all your possessions!
column2: 這是你的所有物品！

column1: It appears open already.
column2: 看起來已經打開了。

column1: The cabinet is closed.
column2: 櫥櫃是關著的。

column1: You have no need of dishes.
column2: 你不需要碗盤。

column1: Why don't you get everything?
column2: 你何不把東西全部拿走？

column1: I don't know how you did it, but you managed to escape Lolotte's henchman. You might not be so lucky next time. Watch Out!!
column2: 不知道你是怎麼辦到的，總之你成功逃過了樂樂蒂手下的追捕。下次可不一定這麼幸運，小心點！！

column1: You see nothing of interest on the cold stone floor of the tower.
column2: 塔內冰冷的石地上沒有什麼引起你的興趣。

column1: The stairs spiral steeply up the stone tower.
column2: 樓梯沿著石塔陡峭地盤旋而上。

column1: The stone steps spiral dangerously upward.
column2: 石階危險地盤旋向上。

column1: You glance quickly through the open doorway, but do not see anything of interest in the adjoining room.
column2: 你迅速瞄了一眼敞開的門，但沒在隔壁房間看到什麼引起你興趣的東西。

column1: You are at the bottom of the west tower. A spiral of stone steps winds to the top.
column2: 你正位於西塔的底部，一道螺旋石階盤旋而上，直達頂端。

column1: This is a rather cheerless dining room. Eating here would NOT be a pleasant experience.
column2: 這是間相當陰鬱的餐廳，在這裡用餐絕不會是件愉快的事。

column1: There is nothing under the table.
column2: 桌子底下什麼都沒有。

column1: The great dining table looks big enough to seat Lolotte and at least eight of her despicable henchmen.
column2: 這張大餐桌看起來夠大，足以容納樂樂蒂和至少八個她那些可惡的手下。

column1: There is nothing but a wall behind the tapestry.
column2: 掛毯後面除了牆什麼都沒有。

column1: There's a most unusual tapestry hanging on the wall.
column2: 牆上掛著一幅相當不尋常的掛毯。

column1: An interesting chandelier above the dining table casts flickering shadows around the room.
column2: 餐桌上方一盞奇特的吊燈，將搖曳的陰影投射在房間四周。

column1: You see three open doorways.
column2: 你看到三扇敞開的門。

column1: Currently, Lolotte's ornate, gilded throne is empty.
column2: 此刻，樂樂蒂那華麗的鎏金王座是空的。

column1: The carpeted stairs lead to Lolotte's gilded throne.
column2: 鋪著地毯的台階通往樂樂蒂的鎏金王座。

column1: There is nothing but wall behind the tapestry.
column2: 掛毯後面除了牆什麼都沒有。

column1: Two strange-looking tapestries hang on the wall behind Lolotte's throne.
column2: 樂樂蒂王座後方的牆上掛著兩幅樣式古怪的掛毯。

column1: You see two open doorways.
column2: 你看到兩扇敞開的門。

column1: The carpet leads to Lolotte's throne.
column2: 這條地毯通往樂樂蒂的王座。

column1: Lolotte's throne room is cold, dark, and ominous. From here, she rules her domain with an evil hand.
column2: 樂樂蒂的王座廳陰冷、黑暗且不祥，她就是在這裡以邪惡之手統治她的領地。

column1: Just go there.
column2: 直接過去就行了。

column1: OH, NO!! The door is locked!
column2: 喔，不！！門鎖住了！

column1: What now, Rosella?!
column2: 羅塞拉，現在該怎麼辦？！

column1: ERROR
column2: 錯誤

column1: Well, my pets, Lolotte hisses, "What have you drug home today?"
column2: 「哦，我的寵物們，」樂樂蒂嘶聲說道，「你們今天又拖了什麼回來？」

column1: Lolotte gives you the once over, and as she does her red eyes begin to narrow.
column2: 樂樂蒂上下打量你一番，她那雙紅眼漸漸瞇了起來。

column1: Are you a foolish girl who wandered here by mistake...or are you a SPY sent here by my enemy, Genesta?!
column2: 「你是個誤闖此地的傻女孩……還是我的敵人吉妮絲派來的間諜？！」

column1: You swear to Lolotte that you are nothing but a poor peasant girl who has lost her way, but she doesn't seem to believe you as her mouth begins to snarl and her eyes narrow to mere slits.
column2: 你向樂樂蒂發誓，自己不過是個迷路的可憐農家女孩，但她似乎不相信你，嘴角開始咧起，雙眼瞇成一條細縫。

column1: For the first time, you experience real fear and begin to tremble.
column2: 你生平第一次感受到真正的恐懼，開始顫抖起來。

column1: Lolotte snarls, "You don't look STUPID enough to have wandered HERE. I believe you're a SPY!"
column2: 樂樂蒂咆哮道：「你看起來還沒笨到會誤闖這裡，我相信你是個間諜！」

column1: Take her to the cell!
column2: 「把她關進牢房！」

column1: Lolotte gives you a sickly-sweet look. "You're lucky, my dear," she coos.
column2: 樂樂蒂露出一副矯情做作的笑容。「你很幸運，親愛的，」她柔聲說道。

column1: My precious son, Edgar, has taken a liking to you, and he believes you. He's convinced me to give you a chance to prove your innocence.
column2: 「我心愛的兒子艾德加喜歡上你了，他相信你。他說服了我，給你一個證明清白的機會。」

column1: You glance at the homely young man standing next to Lolotte. He blushes.
column2: 你瞥了一眼站在樂樂蒂身旁那位其貌不揚的青年，他臉紅了。

column1: Lolotte continues, "I wish to own the unicorn that inhabits the meadowland."
column2: 樂樂蒂接著說：「我想要擁有那隻棲息在草原上的獨角獸。」

column1: Bring me the unicorn, and I shall not only set you free, but reward you as well.
column2: 「把獨角獸帶來給我，我不僅會放你自由，還會給你獎賞。」

column1: To her henchmen, Lolotte orders, "Go! Take her back to the forest!"
column2: 樂樂蒂對她的手下下令：「去！把她帶回森林！」

column1: So, Lolotte says, "I hear you were a good girl and brought me the unicorn."
column2: 「這麼說，」樂樂蒂說道，「我聽說你是個乖女孩，把獨角獸帶來給我了。」

column1: I'm ALMOST convinced you might be innocent after all. But... she clears her throat, "not quite."
column2: 「我幾乎要相信你其實是清白的了。但是……」她清了清喉嚨，「還差一點。」

column1: Oh, oh. This doesn't sound good, you think.
column2: 糟了，你心想，這聽起來不妙。

column1: Lolotte continues, "I am desirous of the hen that lays the golden eggs. Currently, the hen is kept by the ogre and his wife."
column2: 樂樂蒂接著說：「我渴望得到那隻會下金蛋的母雞，牠目前由那對食人魔夫婦養著。」

column1: Bring me the hen, and you shall have your freedom and a great reward!
column2: 「把母雞帶來給我，你就能獲得自由和豐厚的獎賞！」

column1: Take her away! Lolotte orders her goons.
column2: 「把她帶下去！」樂樂蒂對她的爪牙下令。

column1: What a good girl you are! Lolotte gushes. "I really ought to give you your reward right now!"
column2: 「你真是個乖女孩！」樂樂蒂滿臉堆笑，「我現在就該給你獎賞才對！」

column1: She pauses, and you wonder what she's up to.
column2: 她停頓了一下，你不禁猜想她在打什麼主意。

column1: Lolotte goes on to say, "But, I have just the tiniest bit of doubt left."
column2: 樂樂蒂接著說：「不過，我心裡還剩下那麼一絲絲懷疑。」

column1: If you do just ONE MORE thing for me, I'll be COMPLETELY convinced of your innocence and you'll receive a wonderful reward!
column2: 「只要你再替我做一件事，我就會完全相信你是清白的，並給你一份豐厚的獎賞！」

column1: Somehow, you don't quite believe her.
column2: 不知怎地，你並不太相信她。

column1: I want to have Pandora's Box. It is said that whoever owns it would be in possession of the purest evil.
column2: 「我想要得到潘朵拉的盒子。據說擁有它的人，就等於擁有最純粹的邪惡力量。」

column1: Lolotte's red eyes glow as she exclaims, "With the power and evil of Pandora's Box, I would be UNSTOPPABLE!"
column2: 樂樂蒂的紅眼閃著光芒，她驚呼：「有了潘朵拉盒子的力量與邪惡，我將無人能擋！」

column1: Your terror returns as you realize what an evil being Lolotte really is!
column2: 你再度感到恐懼，這才明白樂樂蒂究竟是個多麼邪惡的存在！

column1: You wonder if you should be helping her in this way. But...what can you do about it? Nothing...right now.
column2: 你不禁懷疑自己是否該這樣幫她。但是……你又能怎麼辦呢？眼下……什麼也做不了。

column1: I don't know the whereabouts of Pandora's Box, Lolotte tells you. "You must search for it yourself."
column2: 「我不知道潘朵拉的盒子在哪裡，」樂樂蒂告訴你，「你得自己去找。」

column1: Again, she orders her henchmen to "Take her away!"
column2: 她再次對手下下令：「把她帶下去！」

column1: Lolotte smiles an evil smile. "You know," she says, "I've never asked you your name. What is it?"
column2: 樂樂蒂露出邪惡的笑容。「你知道嗎，」她說，「我從沒問過你的名字，妳叫什麼？」

column1: You tell her "Rosella."
column2: 你告訴她：「羅塞拉。」

column1: Rosella. What a pretty name. You wonder what this is all about.
column2: 「羅塞拉，好美的名字。」你猜不透這究竟是怎麼一回事。

column1: You find out soon enough. "Well, Rosella," Lolotte grins ominously, "You've earned your reward!"
column2: 你很快就明白了。「好了，羅塞拉，」樂樂蒂露出不祥的笑容，「你已經賺到你的獎賞了！」

column1: My son, Edgar, has fallen in love with you. I wish him to be happy, and I give you the honor of being his wife and MY daughter-in-law!
column2: 「我兒子艾德加已經愛上你了。我希望他幸福，所以我要賜你這份榮耀，成為他的妻子、我的媳婦！」

column1: Your heart turns flips and somersaults as you hear this.
column2: 聽到這句話，你的心翻騰不已。

column1: You'll be married first thing in the morning, she informs you.
column2: 「你們明天一早就要成親，」她這麼告訴你。

column1: You will sleep in Edgar's room tonight, while my son sleeps elsewhere.
column2: 「今晚你睡在艾德加的房間，我兒子則睡到別處去。」

column1: She motions to her goons. "Let us relieve you of your burdensome load before you retire."
column2: 她朝她的爪牙示意。「讓我們先卸下你身上的累贅，你再去休息。」

column1: You are so shocked by this tragic turn of events, that you hardly notice your possessions being taken away.
column2: 這場悲慘的變故讓你震驚不已，幾乎沒注意到自己的物品被拿走了。

column1: To her goons, Lolotte orders, "Escort our Rosella to her room."
column2: 樂樂蒂對她的爪牙下令：「護送我們的羅塞拉回房。」

column1: You are at the bottom of the east tower. A spiral of stone steps winds to the top.
column2: 你正位於東塔的底部，一道螺旋石階盤旋而上，直達頂端。

column1: Looking up, you see nothing special.
column2: 你抬頭一看，沒發現什麼特別的東西。

column1: Rosella! You're standing in the way!
column2: 羅塞拉！妳擋到路了！

column1: The unicorn rolls back its eyes. It obviously doesn't trust you any more.
column2: 獨角獸的眼神往後一縮，顯然牠已經不再信任你了。

column1: The unicorn doesn't want you to.
column2: 獨角獸不願意讓你這麼做。

column1: What unicorn?
column2: 什麼獨角獸？

column1: The unicorn looks at you with sadness.
column2: 獨角獸用哀傷的眼神看著你。

column1: It needs to be free...
column2: 牠需要獲得自由……

column1: The unicorn is not here.
column2: 獨角獸不在這裡。

column1: You reach out to pet the unicorn's nose, but it won't let you.
column2: 你伸手想摸摸獨角獸的鼻子，但牠不讓你碰。

column1: The unicorn doesn't trust you; it backs away.
column2: 獨角獸不信任你，牠往後退了開去。

column1: You should free the unicorn.
column2: 你應該把獨角獸放走。

column1: Try opening the gate.
column2: 試著打開柵門。

column1: That would not be a good idea.
column2: 這可不是個好主意。

column1: You see two stable gates.
column2: 你看到兩扇馬廄的柵門。

column1: You see nothing of importance on the walls.
column2: 牆上沒有什麼重要的東西。

column1: You have entered Lolotte's stable... and, it looks like an ordinary stable at that.
column2: 你走進了樂樂蒂的馬廄……看起來就是間普通的馬廄。

column1: The poor unicorn has been imprisoned here!!
column2: 可憐的獨角獸被囚禁在這裡！！

column1: As soon as you open the gate, the splendid wild unicorn impatiently leaves its hated prison. It quickly trots out the open stable door to freedom.
column2: 你一打開柵門，那匹美麗的野生獨角獸便迫不及待地離開牠所厭惡的牢籠，飛快地跑出敞開的馬廄門，奔向自由。

column1: The seagull has no interest in it
column2: 海鷗對此毫無興趣

column1: You don't see any fish right now.
column2: 你現在沒看到任何魚。

column1: The pier is old and weather-beaten.
column2: 這座碼頭老舊又飽經風霜。

column1: Enter the water first.
column2: 先下水吧。

column1: You look under the water, and see...more water!
column2: 你往水下看去，看到的是……更多的水！

column1: There is nothing of interest in the wooden barrel.
column2: 木桶裡沒有什麼引起你的興趣。

column1: The wide, blue ocean disappears into the horizon to the west.
column2: 遼闊的藍色海洋在西方消失於地平線。

column1: It's just an ordinary seagull.
column2: 這只是隻普通的海鷗。

column1: You see an old rotted rope attached to the pier.
column2: 你看到碼頭上繫著一條老舊腐朽的繩子。

column1: You see a grizzled, old fisherman on the pier. Apparently, he isn't having any luck today.
column2: 你看到碼頭上有位鬚髮花白的老漁夫，看樣子他今天手氣不太好。

column1: You don't see him here.
column2: 你在這裡沒看到他。

column1: You are standing at the end of an old weathered pier. From here, to the west, you can see nothing but ocean.
column2: 你站在一座風化老舊碼頭的盡頭。從這裡往西望去，除了汪洋大海什麼也看不見。

column1: You are swimming near the end of an old weathered pier. From here, to the west, you can see nothing but ocean.
column2: 你正在一座風化老舊碼頭盡頭附近游泳。從這裡往西望去，除了汪洋大海什麼也看不見。

column1: You can't when you're on the pier.
column2: 你在碼頭上沒辦法這麼做。

column1: You taste the ocean water. YUCK!! This water's too salty!
column2: 你嚐了嚐海水。噁！這水也太鹹了吧！

column1: Just jump on in!
column2: 直接跳下去就對了！

column1: The water is much too deep here.
column2: 這裡的水太深了。

column1: You wouldn't want it. It's of no use to you.
column2: 你不會想要那個的，對你沒有用處。

column1: Not while you're swimming.
column2: 你游泳的時候不能這麼做。

column1: What do you want to do with it?
column2: 你想拿它做什麼？

column1: The seagull is not interested.
column2: 海鷗對此沒興趣。

column1: You can't kiss a seagull.
column2: 你不能親吻海鷗。

column1: Seagulls don't talk.
column2: 海鷗不會說話。

column1: You can't catch the seagull.
column2: 你抓不到那隻海鷗。

column1: You don't see him.
column2: 你沒看到他。

column1: You think for a moment that it might help, but then change your mind.
column2: 你想了一下，覺得這樣或許有幫助，但隨即又改變了主意。

column1: There is no one nearby.
column2: 附近沒有人。

column1: Eagerly, from the end of the pier, you fling the baited line of the fishing pole into the ocean.
column2: 你興沖沖地站在碼頭盡頭，把裝了魚餌的釣魚線甩進海裡。

column1: Almost immediately, you feel a sharp tug on the line! Something pulls and fights your line as you slowly reel it in. There it is! You have caught yourself...a fine fat fish!
column2: 幾乎是一瞬間，你感覺到釣線猛地一沉！有東西在線的另一端拉扯掙扎，你慢慢把線收回來。就是牠了！你釣到了……一條肥美的大魚！

column1: Eagerly, from the end of the pier, you fling the line of the fishing pole into the ocean.
column2: 你興沖沖地站在碼頭盡頭，把釣魚線甩進海裡。

column1: But wait! You haven't baited your hook! Feeling rather sheepish, you reel in your empty fishing line.
column2: 等等！你根本沒裝魚餌！你有點不好意思地把空空如也的釣魚線收了回來。

column1: Almost immediately, you feel a sharp tug on the line! Something pulls and fights your line as you slowly reel it in. There it is! You have caught yourself...an old waterlogged boot. But, since you have no need of a boot, you toss it back into the water.
column2: 幾乎是一瞬間，你感覺到釣線猛地一沉！有東西在線的另一端拉扯掙扎，你慢慢把線收回來。就是牠了！你釣到的是……一隻泡爛的舊靴子。不過你用不著靴子，就把它丟回水裡了。

column1: The fish don't seem to be biting.
column2: 魚兒似乎不太上鉤。

column1: With the return of his long-lost son, Alexander, and the rescue of his daughter, Rosella, from the terrible dragon, old King Graham decides it's time to pass on his adventurer's hat to younger blood.
column2: 隨著失散多年的兒子亞歷山大歸來，女兒羅塞拉也從可怕的巨龍手中獲救，年邁的卡拉漢國王決定，是時候把冒險者的帽子傳給年輕一輩了。

column1: He flings the battered hat toward his children while his wife, Queen Valanice, proudly looks on.
column2: 他把那頂破舊的帽子拋向孩子們，一旁的蘭妮絲王后則驕傲地看著這一幕。

column1: The hat arches through the air.
column2: 帽子劃過一道弧線，飛向空中。

column1: Suddenly...!
column2: 突然……！

column1: King Graham experiences a terrible squeezing pain in his chest.
column2: 卡拉漢國王的胸口忽然一陣劇烈絞痛。

column1: Help me, he rasps.
column2: 「救救我，」他嘶啞地說。

column1: The adventurer's hat lies, unclaimed, upon the floor...forgotten.
column2: 那頂冒險者的帽子，無人拾起，被遺忘在地板上……

column1: King Graham lies weakly in bed, Father Death hovering near.
column2: 卡拉漢國王虛弱地躺在床上，死神已在一旁徘徊。

column1: Grief suddenly overwhelming her, Rosella runs from the room.
column2: 悲痛突然湧上心頭，羅塞拉奪門而出，跑出了房間。

column1: Oh, father! she sobs. "You're still young; you should have many years ahead of you! Oh, I wish I could help you, Father!"
column2: 「喔，父王！」她哭喊著。「您還這麼年輕，本該還有好多年可以活啊！喔，真希望我能幫幫您，父王！」

column1: Do you really mean that? a soft voice asks.
column2: 「妳是真心這麼想的嗎？」一個輕柔的聲音問道。

column1: Rosella looks up but sees no one. "Who's speaking to me?"
column2: 羅塞拉抬起頭，卻沒看見任何人。「是誰在跟我說話？」

column1: I am, the voice says. "Look in the magic mirror."
column2: 「是我，」那聲音說。「看看魔鏡吧。」

column1: Rosella sees an image in the mirror. "Wh-who are you?" she queries.
column2: 羅塞拉在鏡中看見一個影像。「妳……妳是誰？」她問道。

column1: I am the fairy, Genesta. In my land of Tamir there is a remarkable tree. This tiny tree needs one hundred years to bear a single fruit.
column2: 「我是仙女吉妮絲。在我的國度塔米亞，有一棵奇妙的樹。這棵小樹要花上一百年才能結出一顆果實。」

column1: But, this is no ordinary fruit; for if a person were to eat it, they would find that good health and well-being would be theirs for many years.
column2: 「但這可不是普通的果實，只要吃下它，就能讓人多年保持健康與活力。」

column1: Rosella is much heartened by this news! "Where is the land of Tamir?"
column2: 這個消息讓羅塞拉大受鼓舞！「塔米亞在哪裡？」

column1: Genesta smiles. "Tamir is very far away. But with my magic, I can bring you here."
column2: 吉妮絲微笑著說：「塔米亞非常遙遠，不過憑我的魔法，可以把妳帶來這裡。」

column1: Rosella senses that there is more to the story. "But, I suppose there are some problems?"
column2: 羅塞拉察覺事情沒那麼簡單。「不過……應該還有什麼難題吧？」

column1: Again, Genesta smiles. "Yes, you are correct, Rosella. If you are willing to come to Tamir I will explain the situation."
column2: 吉妮絲再次微笑。「沒錯，妳猜對了，羅塞拉。如果妳願意來塔米亞，我會向妳解釋一切。」

column1: However, once I bring you here, I can't send you back; you will have to help me first.
column2: 「不過，一旦我把妳帶來這裡，就沒辦法送妳回去了，妳得先幫我一個忙才行。」

column1: Confused, Rosella says, "I don't know! What if I can't help you; or find the tree?"
column2: 羅塞拉一臉困惑地說：「我不知道耶！萬一我幫不了妳，或是找不到那棵樹怎麼辦？」

column1: Genesta pleads, "You must decide NOW, Rosella. My powers are growing weaker by the minute."
column2: 吉妮絲懇求道：「妳現在就得做出決定，羅塞拉。我的法力正一分一秒地衰弱下去。」

column1: Now, the fairy is but a faint glimmer, and her voice barely audible. "If you care for your father, say `yes' now!..." And suddenly she's gone.
column2: 此刻，仙女只剩下一絲微弱的光芒，聲音也幾乎聽不見了。「如果妳在乎妳的父親，現在就說『願意』吧！……」話音未落，她便消失無蹤。

column1: YES!!
state:%2d music:%3d highest:%3d
column2: 狀態:%2d 音樂:%3d 最高:%3d

column1: The lovely fairy speaks. "I am the fairy, Genesta. Welcome to Tamir!"
column2: 美麗的仙女開口了。「我是仙女吉妮絲，歡迎來到塔米亞！」

column1: Rosella looks awestruck at the fairy. She is stunningly beautiful! But still there is a certain sadness to her.
column2: 羅塞拉滿懷敬畏地望著這位仙女。她美得令人屏息！但眉宇間卻透著一絲揮之不去的哀傷。

column1: Finally, Rosella speaks. "I know you would like me to help you in some way, but I don't know how I could help a fairy."
column2: 羅塞拉終於開口了。「我知道妳希望我能幫上什麼忙，但我實在不知道自己要怎麼幫助一位仙女。」

column1: Genesta looks sadder than ever. "You're wrong, Rosella. You see, I'm losing my magical powers. Yesterday as I was strolling through the woods, Lolotte, the evil fairy, caught me unawares and stole my magic talisman."
column2: 吉妮絲的神情比以往更加哀傷。「妳錯了，羅塞拉。妳看，我的法力正逐漸消失。昨天我在林間散步時，邪惡仙女樂樂蒂趁我不備，偷走了我的魔法護身符。」

column1: The fairy sighs, "She yanked it from my neck and raced away screeching with laughter. Immediately I felt my powers diminishing and my body weakening. I will die in 24 hours if my talisman is not returned."
column2: 仙女嘆了口氣：「她從我脖子上一把扯下護身符，尖笑著飛奔而去。我立刻感覺到法力衰退、身體虛弱。要是護身符不能物歸原主，我將在二十四小時內死去。」

column1: Intently, Genesta looks at Rosella. "Lolotte is very evil and will use the talisman to bring more evil to Tamir. Now I fear it will contaminate my whole country!"
column2: 吉妮絲目不轉睛地望著羅塞拉。「樂樂蒂心腸狠毒，會利用護身符把更多邪惡帶進塔米亞。我擔心整個國家都會因此淪陷！」

column1: Further, I cannot send you home without my talisman.
column2: 「而且，沒有護身符，我也沒辦法送妳回家。」

column1: Rosella is unsure as to what SHE can do. Meekly, she asks, "I want to help you, but how?"
column2: 羅塞拉不確定自己能做些什麼。她怯生生地問：「我想幫妳，但要怎麼做呢？」

column1: You can do more than you think, Rosella, Genesta assures her. "I believe you will be able to penetrate Lolotte's domain."
column2: 「妳能做到的，比妳想像中更多，羅塞拉，」吉妮絲安撫她。「我相信妳有辦法潛入樂樂蒂的地盤。」

column1: Suddenly, Rosella remembers the tiny tree. "Can you tell me where to find the magic fruit?"
column2: 羅塞拉忽然想起那棵小樹。「妳能告訴我上哪找那顆魔法果實嗎？」

column1: Remorsefully, Genesta looks at Rosella. "It will not be easy to reach the tree. It grows on a tiny island within a vast swamp on the other side of the great mountains."
column2: 吉妮絲滿懷歉意地看著羅塞拉。「要抵達那棵樹並不容易，它長在群山另一側一片廣袤沼澤中的小島上。」

column1: Bravely fighting back tears, Rosella says, "I will help you in any way I can, Genesta. How can I find Lolotte?"
column2: 羅塞拉強忍淚水，勇敢地說：「我會盡我所能幫助妳，吉妮絲。我該怎麼找到樂樂蒂？」

column1: The beautiful fairy points eastward. "Lolotte's castle overlooks Tamir from the great mountains."
column2: 美麗的仙女指向東方。「樂樂蒂的城堡矗立在群山之巔，俯瞰著整個塔米亞。」

column1: Genesta looks weaker as she says, "There is not much more I can do, Rosella. As it is, it will be difficult for me to fly home again."
column2: 吉妮絲的神情更加虛弱，她說：「我能做的已經不多了，羅塞拉。就連飛回家，恐怕都很吃力。」

column1: One thing I must do for you, though. I shall disguise you as a peasant girl so as not to attract attention.
column2: 「不過有一件事我一定得為妳做。我會把妳偽裝成一名農家女孩，免得引人注目。」

column1: Th-thank-you, Genesta...I think, Rosella stammers.
column2: 「謝……謝謝妳，吉妮絲……大概吧，」羅塞拉結結巴巴地說。

column1: It will be better for you, the fairy says. "Well, I must be off while I can still fly. I KNOW you can do it!"
column2: 「這樣對妳比較好，」仙女說。「好了，趁我還能飛，我得走了。我相信妳一定辦得到！」

column1: Good-bye, good luck.
column2: 「再見了，祝妳好運。」

column1: Well, you're on your own, Rosella.
column2: 好了，接下來就靠妳自己了，羅塞拉。

column1: Got Here
column2: 到達此地

column1: POOF!! You're a little froggy!
column2: 噗！你變成一隻小青蛙了！

column1: You don't like the clammy feeling of being a frog. You look around, decide there's no reason to be one right now, and remove the little gold crown.
column2: 你不喜歡當青蛙那種濕黏黏的感覺。你環顧四周，覺得現在沒必要維持這個樣子，於是摘下了那頂小金冠。

column1: Your 24 hours are up! Unfortunately for you and Tamir, both Genesta and your father, King Graham, have died. It looks like you're destined to stay here in Tamir forever; AND evil will rule the land. What a shame!
column2: 你的二十四小時到了！不幸的是，對妳和塔米亞來說，吉妮絲和妳的父親卡拉漢國王都已經去世了。看來妳注定要永遠留在塔米亞了，而且邪惡勢力將統治這片土地。真是太遺憾了！

column1: You shoot an arrow into the air...
column2: 你朝天空射出一支箭……

column1: Oops!...you missed!
column2: 哎呀！……你沒射中！

column1: In thy face I see The map of honor, truth, and loyalty.
column2: 我從你的面容上，看見了榮譽、真誠與忠誠的印記。

column1: Forbear to judge, for we are sinners all. Close up his eyes and draw the curtain close; And let us all to meditation.
column2: 且莫論斷，因為我們都是罪人。闔上他的雙眼，拉起帷幕；讓我們都靜心沉思吧。

column1: No beast so fierce but knows some touch of pity.
column2: 再兇猛的野獸，也懂得幾分憐憫之心。

column1: Look, how my ring encompasseth thy finger, Even so thy breast encloseth my poor heart; Wear both of them, for both of them are thine.
column2: 看哪，我的戒指如何環繞著妳的手指，妳的心懷也同樣擁抱著我這顆可憐的心；請兩者都戴上吧，因為它們都屬於妳。

column1: The world is grown so bad, That wrens make prey where eagles dare not perch.
column2: 這世道已敗壞至此，連鷦鷯都敢在老鷹不敢棲息之處捕食。

column1: O, I have passed a miserable night, So full of ugly sights, of ghastly dreams, That, as I am a Christian faithful man, I would not spend another such a night, Though 'twere to buy a world of happy days.
column2: 唉，我度過了悽慘的一夜，滿是醜惡的景象與駭人的惡夢，身為一個虔誠的基督徒，就算能換來滿世界的幸福時光，我也不願再熬過這樣一夜。

column1: Is the chair empty? Is the sword unswayed? Is the king dead? The empire unpossessed?
column2: 王座空了嗎？寶劍無人揮動了嗎？國王駕崩了嗎？帝國無主了嗎？

column1: A horse! A horse! My kingdom for a horse!
column2: 一匹馬！一匹馬！用我的王國換一匹馬！

column1: Tut! I have done a thousand dreadful things As willingly as one would kill a fly.
column2: 哼！我做過上千件可怕的事，就像捏死一隻蒼蠅般毫不在意。

column1: Kiss me, Kate, we will be married o' Sunday.
column2: 吻我吧，凱特，我們星期天就成婚。

column1: He jests at scars, that never felt a wound. But, soft! what light through yonder window breaks? It is the east, and Juliet is the sun!
column2: 從未受過傷的人，才會拿傷疤來開玩笑。且慢！那邊窗中透出的是什麼光？那是東方，而茱麗葉就是太陽！

column1: See! how she leans her cheek upon her hand: O! that I were a glove upon that hand, That I might touch that cheek.
column2: 看哪！她的臉頰倚在手上：喔！但願我是那隻手上的手套，好讓我能觸碰那臉頰。

column1: O Romeo, Romeo! wherefore art thou Romeo? Deny thy father, and refuse thy name; Or, if thou wilt not, be but sworn my love, And I'll no longer be a Capulet.
column2: 喔，羅密歐，羅密歐！你為何是羅密歐？否認你的父親，捨棄你的姓氏吧；若你不願，只要你立誓愛我，我便不再是卡布雷特家的人。

column1: What's in a name? That which we call a rose by any other name would smell as sweet.
column2: 名字又代表什麼呢？我們稱之為玫瑰的花，就算換了別的名字，依然芬芳如故。

column1: This bud of love, by summer's ripening breath, May prove a beauteous flower when next we meet.
column2: 這朵愛情的花苞，在夏日暖風的吹拂下，待我們再會之時，或將綻放成一朵美麗的花。

column1: Good night, good night! Parting is such sweet sorrow, That I shall say good night till it be morrow.
column2: 晚安，晚安！離別是如此甜蜜的哀愁，我願道一聲聲晚安，直到天明。

column1: Of comfort no man speak: Let's talk of graves, of worms, and epitaphs; Make dust our paper, and with rainy eyes Write sorrow on the bosom of the earth; Let's choose executors and talk of wills.
column2: 莫再談安慰之詞：讓我們談談墳墓、蛆蟲與墓誌銘吧；以塵土為紙，含淚在大地的胸膛上寫下哀傷；讓我們選定遺囑執行人，談談身後之事。

column1: You may my glories and my state depose, But not my griefs; still am I king of those.
column2: 你可以剝奪我的榮耀與王位，卻奪不走我的悲傷；那片國度，我依然是王。

column1: How sour sweet music is when time is broke and no proportion kept! So is it in the music of men's lives.
column2: 當節拍錯亂、韻律盡失，再甜美的音樂也變得刺耳！人生的樂章亦是如此。

column1: Love looks not with the eyes, but with the mind, And therefore is winged Cupid painted blind.
column2: 愛不是用眼睛看的，而是用心去感受，因此丘比特才被畫成一個蒙著眼的孩子。

column1: I am that merry wanderer of the night. I jest to Oberon, and make him smile When I a fat and bean-fed horse beguile, Neighing in likeness of a filly foal: And sometimes lurk I in a gossip's bowl, In very likeness of a roasted crab.
column2: 我就是那夜裡快活的漫遊者。我逗奧伯龍開心，學小母馬嘶鳴，把肥壯的馬兒騙得團團轉；有時我還會化身烤蘋果，潛伏在長舌婦的酒碗裡。

column1: You spotted snakes with double tongue, Thorny hedge-hogs, be not seen; Newts, and blind-worms, do no wrong; Come not near our fairy queen.
column2: 生著雙叉舌的斑紋毒蛇，渾身是刺的刺蝟，都別出現；蠑螈與蚓蜥，切莫作祟；都別靠近我們的仙后。

column1: How oft the sight of means to do ill deeds Makes ill deeds done!
column2: 作惡的機會擺在眼前，往往就促成了惡行的發生！

column1: I am amazed, methinks, and lose my way Among the thorns and dangers of this world.
column2: 我想，我如今茫然失措，在這世間的荊棘與險境中迷失了方向。

column1: The day shall not be up so soon as I, To try the fair adventure of tomorrow.
column2: 明日的太陽升起之前，我便已啟程，去嘗試那場美好的冒險。

column1: How far that little candle throws his beams! So shines a good deed in a naughty world!
column2: 一支小小的蠟燭，竟能照亮這麼遠！一件善行，在這醜惡的世間也是如此閃耀！

column1: Bell, book and candle shall not drive me back.
column2: 鐘聲、聖經與燭火，都無法將我驅退。

column1: To hold, as 'twere, the mirror up to nature; to show virtue her own feature, scorn her own image, and the very age and body of the time his form and pressure.
column2: 彷彿舉起一面鏡子映照自然；讓美德看清自己的容貌，讓醜惡看清自己的模樣，也讓這個時代看清自身的樣態與本質。

column1: The lady doth protest too much, methinks.
column2: 我看那位夫人辯解得未免太過頭了。

column1: 'Tis now the very witching time of night, When churchyards yawn and hell itself breathes out Contagion to this world.
column2: 此刻正是夜半的魔魅時分，墳場裂開大口，地獄本身也向這世間吐出瘟疫般的邪氣。

column1: Help! The girls in "Leisure-Suit Larry in the Land of the Lounge Lizards" are mighty uncooperative. Give me some help here, guys!
column2: 救命啊！《Leisure-Suit Larry in the Land of the Lounge Lizards》裡的女孩們超不配合的。各位，幫幫忙啊！

column1: I see a beautiful mermaid in "King's Quest II," but she won't help me! Tell me what to do!
column2: 我在《King's Quest II》裡看到一位美麗的美人魚，但她不肯幫我！快告訴我該怎麼辦！

column1: Anybody out there! I had a condor drop this bottle in the nearest ocean to beg for help with "King's Quest I." Help me fight this fire-breathing dragon!
column2: 外面有人嗎！我請一隻兀鷹把這個瓶子丟進最近的海洋，向大家求救，我在玩《King's Quest I》。幫我對付這頭噴火巨龍吧！

column1: Help! I'm about to get blasted by evil Sariens in "Space Quest!" Anybody have a handy ray gun out there?!
column2: 救命！我快被《Space Quest》裡邪惡的薩利安人轟成灰了！誰那邊剛好有把雷射槍啊？！

column1: Sludge Vohaul is holding me prisoner in "Space Quest II!" Help me out of this jam!
column2: 史勒吉·沃霍爾把我關起來了，我人在《Space Quest II》裡！快幫我脫困！

column1: Drug dealers are terrorizing the city of Lytton! I need a partner to help put away these bad guys in "Police Quest!"

column1: Hey, I need help here! The wicked Horned King has had me thrown into his dungeon! If I don't get out of here, then evil will rule the world in "The Black Cauldron!"
column2: 嘿，我這裡需要幫忙！邪惡的角族之王把我扔進他的地牢了！要是我出不去，《The Black Cauldron》裡的邪惡勢力就要統治世界了！

column1: Help me! I'm lost at sea in "King's Quest III!" I'm with a bunch of nasty pirates who want to feed me to the sharks! If you can, come quick!
column2: 救救我！我在《King's Quest III》裡在海上迷航了！身邊還有一群兇惡的海盜想把我餵鯊魚！可以的話，快點來救我！

column1: I'm a little kid, and my name's Tommy. I'm having trouble with Old King Cole and Humpty Dumpty in "Mixed-Up Mother Goose!" Come and help me!
column2: 我是個小孩子，我叫湯米。我在《Mixed-Up Mother Goose》裡被老王高爾和蛋頭先生弄得焦頭爛額！快來幫幫我吧！

column1: Oh, no! Help me!! I'm stranded on an airplane with a guy named Ken, being chased by the KGB and an evil army of henchettes, former church secretaries, game show hostesses, and bimbos. Buy "Leisure Suit Larry Goes Looking For Love (in Several Wrong Places.)"
column2: 喔，不！救命啊！！我被困在一架飛機上，身邊還有個叫肯的傢伙，後面追著KGB特務和一整支由打手、前教會秘書、遊戲節目主持人和花瓶美女組成的邪惡大軍。快去買《Leisure Suit Larry Goes Looking For Love (in Several Wrong Places.)》吧。

column1: Ok, but you'll be sorry!
column2: 好吧，但你會後悔的！

column1: That was very foolish, Rosella! Now look what you've gone and done! You've loosed the terrible demons...and killed yourself in the process!!
column2: 這實在太傻了，羅塞拉！瞧瞧妳做了什麼好事！妳放出了可怕的惡魔……還把自己給害死了！！

column1: The unicorn seems paralyzed with fear at the sight of Lolotte's henchmen.
column2: 獨角獸一看見樂樂蒂的手下，似乎就嚇得動彈不得。

column2: 湛藍的海水靜靜拍打著這片美麗的沙灘。

column2: 眼前明亮湛藍的天空，彷彿與浩瀚的海洋融為一體。

column1: You see nothing but sandy soil.
column2: 你只看見一片沙土。

column1: There are not many rocks here.
column2: 這裡的石頭不多。

column2: 東邊的草地上，點綴著一叢叢低矮的灌木。

column1: Beautiful wildflowers adorn the green meadowland to the east.
column2: 東邊翠綠的草地上，開滿了美麗的野花。

column2: 你游泳的時候沒辦法釣魚。

column2: 這裡不是個釣魚的好地方。

column2: 海鷗似乎沒興趣。

column2: 你不能丟那個。

column1: That would accomplish nothing.

column1: Something catches your eye! Why, it's a beautiful peacock feather!
column2: 有東西吸引了你的目光！哎呀，是一根漂亮的孔雀羽毛！

column1: The ocean water laps gently upon the sandy beach of the island.
column2: 海水輕輕拍打著島上的沙灘。

column1: You see a beautiful peacock feather lying upon the beach.

column1: You don't see many rocks.

column1: Lush green grass carpets this magical island.
column2: 蒼翠的綠草覆蓋著這座神奇的島嶼。

column1: A low hedge borders the lovely garden.
column2: 一道低矮的樹籬圍繞著這座美麗的花園。

column1: The foliage is lush and green.
column2: 枝葉繁茂翠綠。

column1: Beautiful flowers seem to flourish here.
column2: 這裡似乎盛開著美麗的花朵。

column2: 一座迷人的象牙宮殿聳立在這座僻靜島嶼的中央，四周環繞著茂密的花園。

column1: A majestic peacock, with a beautiful tail, struts by.
column2: 一隻雄偉的孔雀拖著美麗的尾羽昂首走過。

column1: You don't see one.
column2: 你沒看到。

column1: Peacocks don't talk!
column2: 孔雀不會說話！

column1: You might find one lying around here somewhere.
column2: 你或許能在附近找到一個。

column1: You don't need the peacock.
column2: 你不需要那隻孔雀。

column1: Small, low bushes dot the pretty meadow. Wildflowers grow among them.
column2: 低矮的小灌木點綴著這片美麗的草原，野花在其間盛開。

column1: Scattered rocks lie among the wildflowers of the meadow.
column2: 零散的岩石散落在草原的野花之間。

column1: You look at the ground and see nothing but green grass and wildflowers.
column2: 你看向地面，只看到綠草和野花。

column1: Green grass carpets this lovely meadowland.
column2: 綠草覆蓋著這片可愛的草原。

column1: Colorful wildflowers grow among the small rocks and bushes of the meadowland.
column2: 五彩的野花在草原的小岩石和灌木間盛開。

column2: 小岩石散落在樹木之間。

column2: 綠草覆蓋著這片樹林。

column1: There are not many bushes in these woods.
column2: 這片樹林裡的灌木不多。

column1: Soft grass cushions your walk through these woods.
column2: 柔軟的草地讓你走過這片樹林時腳步輕鬆。

column1: You don't notice many flowers here.
column2: 你沒注意到這裡有很多花。

column1: The trees are numerous in these woods.
column2: 這片樹林裡樹木眾多。

column1: The raven doesn't look to be a friendly bird at all!
column2: 這隻烏鴉看起來一點也不友善！

column1: You see small birds here and there.
column2: 你四處看到一些小鳥。

column1: There aren't many flowers to pick!
column2: 沒有多少花可以摘！

column1: It is difficult to climb trees while wearing that dress!.
column2: 穿著那件洋裝很難爬樹！。

column1: Caw! Caw!
column2: 呱！呱！

column1: You wouldn't want it even if you COULD get it!
column2: 就算你拿得到，你也不會想要！

column1: Not this bird!
column2: 不是這隻鳥！

column1: Pine needles, among the grass, crunch beneath your feet as you walk through the forest.
column2: 走過森林時，草叢間的松針在你腳下發出沙沙聲。

column1: You don't see many shrubs here.
column2: 這裡的灌木不多。

column1: Mostly, you see pine trees.
column2: 這裡大多是松樹。

column1: A forest of pine trees encircles you.
column2: 一片松樹林將你團團圍住。

column1: Caw, caw!
The rocks are insignificant here.
column2: 這裡的岩石不起眼。

column1: Despite this awful forest, the sky is quite nice and blue.
column2: 儘管這片森林陰森可怕，天空倒是晴朗湛藍。

column1: There is little grass here.
column2: 這裡的草很少。

column1: All you see are these awful trees!
column2: 你只看到這些陰森的樹！

column1: There are not many flowers here.
column2: 這裡的花不多。

column1: These are vicious looking trees, aren't they? Best to stay clear of them!
column2: 這些樹看起來很兇惡，對吧？最好離它們遠一點！

column1: You'd swear that these trees were LOOKING at you...! But that's impossible.
column2: 你敢發誓這些樹正盯著你看……！但那不可能。

column1: There are no flowers here!
column2: 這裡沒有花！

column1: You've GOT to be KIDDING!!
column2: 你一定是在開玩笑吧！！

column1: That would be impossible for you to do.
column2: 你不可能做到那件事。

column1: That's not necessary anymore.
column2: 現在已經沒必要了。

column1: How would you go about doing that?
column2: 你打算怎麼做到那件事？

column1: Go ahead, try it.
column2: 去吧，試試看。

column1: These trees do not have the capability to talk.
column2: 這些樹不會說話。

column1: The scary trees only want YOU!
column2: 這些可怕的樹只想要你！

column2: 這些樹在你的威勢下顫抖，被你的斧頭嚇得不敢再騷擾你。

column2: 這裡唯一重要的石頭是墓碑。

column2: 你看到地上有許多墳墓。

column1: The bushes are small and scrubby.
column2: 這些灌木又小又稀疏。

column1: There are few flowers here.
column2: 這裡的花很少。

column1: A pine forest surrounds the decrepit cemetery.
column2: 一片松樹林環繞著這座殘破的墓園。

column2: 這座古老的墓園已經破敗不堪。

column2: 一些墳墓上立著巨大的石碑。

column1: Watch out! Frightful zombies are everywhere!
column2: 小心！到處都是可怕的殭屍！

column1: You don't see one now.
column2: 你現在沒看到。

column2: 停下來摘花只會浪費時間。

column2: 沒有理由再挖了。

column2: 這些墓碑太重了，你搬不動。

column1: The tombstones are firmly planted in the ground.
column2: 這些墓碑牢牢地插在地上。

column1: Trying to reason with a zombie is NOT a good idea!
column2: 想跟殭屍講道理可不是個好主意！

column1: How could you possibly kill something that is ALREADY DEAD!!
column2: 你怎麼可能殺死一個早就死了的東西！！

column1: There are no rocks suitable for climbing here.
column2: 這裡沒有適合攀爬的岩石。

column1: Come now. Would you really want a zombie?
column2: 別鬧了，你真的會想要一隻殭屍嗎？

column1: PLEASE!! Wise up, will you?!!
column2: 拜託！！放聰明點好嗎？！！

column1: You don't want to give anything to a zombie!
column2: 你可不會想給殭屍任何東西！

column1: The rocky mountains rise sharply to the east.
column2: 崎嶇的山脈在東方陡然聳立。

column1: These craggy mountains are much too steep to climb.
column2: 這些嶙峋的山峰太陡峭了，無法攀爬。

column1: You peer into the rushing river and see nothing but sand and pebbles.
column2: 你望向湍急的河水，只看到沙子和鵝卵石。

column1: The cold water of the river rushes westward.
column2: 冰冷的河水向西奔流。

column2: 這條河太淺，不能游泳。

column1: Just enter the river.
column2: 直接進入河裡就行了。

column1: Ahhhh! It tastes good!
column2: 啊∼真好喝！

column1: You kneel down and take a drink of the cool river water. Ahhhh! It tastes good!
column2: 你跪下來喝了一口清涼的河水。啊∼真好喝！

column1: Just enter the swamp if you wish to try that.
column2: 如果你想試試，直接進入沼澤就行了。

column1: Yech!! It tastes AWFUL!
column2: 噁！！味道糟透了！

column1: The water is too murky.
column2: 這水太混濁了。

column1: The mucky swamp spreads in all directions.
column2: 泥濘的沼澤向四面八方延伸。

column1: The steep mountain rises sharply to the west.
column2: 陡峭的山脈在西方拔地而起。

column1: The ground is mostly covered with a swampy quagmire. You notice tufts of swamp grass poking up from the murky water.
column2: 地面大多覆蓋著泥濘的沼澤地，你注意到一叢叢沼澤草從混濁的水中探出頭來。

column1: You notice some grass tufts leading eastward.
column2: 你注意到一叢叢野草通向東方。
